title: CLAUDE.md
text: clade R package providing agent-based evolutionary simulation Julia backend. R user-facing interface (specs, runs, analysis, plots); per-tick simulation kernel lives Julia called per run_alife() via JuliaConnectoR, RтЖФя╕ОJulia boundary crossed per run, per tick. Key facts: - R package layout (DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, R/, man/, tests/testthat/, vignettes/). - Julia kernel inst/julia/ (Project.toml/Manifest.toml, sources inst/julia/src/). - Cached simulation outputs Rdata/*.rds (used vignettes/figures render without re-running). - pkgdown site config: _pkgdown.yml. Docs published https://itchyshin.github.io/clade/.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R-side (run repo root): Quick smoke run: Julia-side (needed kernel debugging):
code: # Load package for interactive dev (no install required) devtools::load_all()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-config.R")  # Rebuild documentation from roxygen comments devtools::document()  # Full check (CRAN-style) devtools::check()  # Build pkgdown site locally (writes to docs/, gitignored) pkgdown::build_site() library(clade) julia_is_ready()         # first call compiles Julia kernel (~60тАУ90 s) specs <- default_specs() specs$n_agents_init <- 40L specs$max_ticks     <- 300L env <- run_alife(specs) plot_run(get_run_data(env)) cd inst/julia julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'

title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896; Simpson 1953) neo-Darwinian mechanism within-lifetime learning can accelerate genetic evolution without Lamarckian inheritance. logic four steps: novel variable environment, genetically fixed behaviours suboptimal. Individuals can adjust behaviour within lifetime survive long enough reproduce. learners reproduce , genetic variants predispose faster deeper learning spread тАФ learning ability becomes selected. generations, fitness benefit learning shrinks genome gradually encodes -learned behaviour directly. endpoint genetic assimilation (Waddington 1942): learned now innate. organism longer needs learn . clade, BNN brain type (brain_type = "bnn") makes process directly observable. agentтАЩs neural network carries prior standard deviation ╧Г synaptic weights. High ╧Г = exploration (wide Thompson sampling, broad behavioural flexibility). Low ╧Г = canalization (narrow prior, behaviour largely determined genome). population mean mean_prior_sigma therefore continuous readout evolution placed population explorationтАУassimilation axis.

title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: Two conditions, 1000 ticks, 150 agents 25├Ч25 grid (grass_rate = 0.10). Two readouts: mean_prior_sigma (assimilation signal тАФ high ╧Г = exploration, low ╧Г = canalization) genetic_diversity (mean pairwise genome distance). Top: mean BNN prior sigma 1000 ticks. Baseline (blue dashed): sigma rises 0.5 exploration ceiling тАФ canalization dynamic foraging world. Epigenetics (orange): sigma declines toward zero TEI propagates within-lifetime learning across generations. Bottom: genetic diversity (mean pairwise genome distance) rises conditions тАФ epigenetic canalization sigma cause selective sweep genome; different genotypes can encode canalized behaviour. Baseline: ╧Г rises ceiling (0.5) stays . foraging landscape shifts continuously тАФ grass grows stochastically, population density creates local competition, single fixed policy consistently outcompetes broad sampling. Exploration evolutionarily stable strategy. Baldwin Effect occur default dynamic world. Epigenetics: ╧Г declines 0.5 toward zero 1000 ticks. mechanism two-step chain: (1) within-lifetime learning contracts agentтАЩs weight posterior lifetime, narrowing ╧Г agent forages; (2) transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) passes narrowed priors offspring. generation starts canalized last, selection reinforces direction. genetic assimilation via Baldwin Effect тАФ achieved Lamarckian-adjacent transmission mechanism (TEI), pure Darwinian selection stable fitness peak. Genetic diversity: conditions show rising diversity 1000 ticks. notable result: epigenetic canalization ╧Г sweep genome. Different weight matrices can encode low-╧Г (canalized) foraging policy, genetic variation maintained even phenotypic trait (╧Г) converges. five systematic experiments ask: canalization occur, disrupts ?
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(patchwork)  base <- default_specs() base$brain_type    <- "bnn" base$n_agents_init <- 150L base$max_ticks     <- 1000L base$grid_rows     <- 25L base$grid_cols     <- 25L base$grass_rate    <- 0.10 base$random_seed   <- 42L # Condition 1: BNN baseline тАФ genetic evolution only env_base <- run_alife(base)  # Condition 2: BNN + epigenetics (TEI) # Within-lifetime learning narrows the BNN prior; TEI transmits the # narrowed priors to offspring. No stable landscape required. s_epi <- base s_epi$epigenetics            <- TRUE s_epi$epigenetic_inheritance <- 0.5 s_epi$methylation_rate       <- 0.2 env_epi <- run_alife(s_epi) df <- bind_rows(   transform(get_run_data(env_base)$ticks, condition = "BNN baseline"),   transform(get_run_data(env_epi)$ticks,  condition = "BNN + epigenetics") ) df$condition <- factor(df$condition,   levels = c("BNN baseline", "BNN + epigenetics"))  pal <- c("BNN baseline"      = "#377eb8",          "BNN + epigenetics" = "#e6550d") lty <- c("BNN baseline"      = "dashed",          "BNN + epigenetics" = "solid")  pA <- ggplot(df, aes(t, mean_prior_sigma, colour = condition, linetype = condition)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "grey70", linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +   scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = lty, name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.55)) +   labs(x = NULL, y = expression("Mean prior " * sigma)) +   theme_classic(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key.width = unit(1.4, "cm"))  pB <- ggplot(df, aes(t, genetic_diversity, colour = condition, linetype = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +   scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = lty, name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity\n(mean pairwise genome distance)") +   theme_classic(base_size = 11) + theme(legend.position = "none")  pA / pB + plot_layout(heights = c(2, 1))

previous_headings: Demonstration
title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: original computational demonstration Baldwin Effect (Hinton & Nowlan 1987) used needle---haystack fitness landscape: one correct genotype gave high fitness, others gave near-zero fitness, learning (random search) provided intermediate fitness signal. Canalization occurred agents correct alleles needed less learning find needle тАФ ╧Г declined correct alleles accumulated. clade provides fixed_patch module implements closest spatial equivalent: permanent high-value resource cell never moves. Agents genetically encode navigation toward patch need less within-lifetime exploration, creating selection pressure high ╧Г. carrying capacity environment must match patch size: 49 patch cells sparse background, starting population 50тАУ80 agents allows selection act without crashing extinction. Parameter tuning (patch radius, grass_rate, n_agents_init) determines whether HintonтАУNowlan result (╧Г declines) population crash occurs тАФ reflecting sensitivity genetic assimilation fitness landscape geometry. five systematic experiments used fixed_patch = FALSE (dynamic world ) map boundary conditions canalization cladeтАЩs default environment.
code: # Hinton & Nowlan (1987) analogue: sparse background, large fixed patch s_hn <- default_specs() s_hn$brain_type         <- "bnn" s_hn$grass_rate         <- 0.02   # sparse тАФ patch becomes essential s_hn$fixed_patch        <- TRUE s_hn$fixed_patch_value  <- 5.0 s_hn$fixed_patch_radius <- 3L     # 7├Ч7 = 49 cells at grid centre s_hn$fixed_patch_x      <- 13L s_hn$fixed_patch_y      <- 13L s_hn$max_ticks          <- 2000L env_hn <- run_alife(s_hn)

what: Experiment 4 тАФ Social modifiers: can they break the ceiling?
title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: Question. genetic evolution within-lifetime RL fail produce canalization, can social transmission mechanisms ? Three candidates: kin-based altruism (energy transfers relatives), prestige-biased social learning (copying successful neighboursтАЩ policy weights), epigenetic inheritance (transgenerational transmission within-lifetime weight contractions). Design. 4 modules ├Ч 2 environments ├Ч 5 seeds ├Ч 1000 ticks = 40 runs. two environments stable-abundant (grass = 0.20, seasonal = 0.0) scarce-stable (grass = 0.05, seasonal = 0.0). found. Stable abundant (grass = 0.20, seasonal = 0): Scarce stable (grass = 0.05, seasonal = 0): Epigenetic inheritance mechanism substantially reduces ╧Г. stable-abundant environment, reduces final ╧Г 0.500 0.342 тАФ 32% ceiling. scarce-stable environment effect stronger: ╧Г reaches 0.140, 72% reduction 0.5 ceiling 65% baseline final value 0.400. Final mean prior sigma social module environment. Epigenetics (red dot) mechanism substantially reduces sigma 0.5 ceiling (dashed line). modules indistinguishable baseline stable environment provide moderate reductions scarce environment. Interpretation. epigenetic mechanism transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI): agent contracts weight posterior lifetime (╧Г narrows learns), offspring inherit partially-contracted priors probability tei_prob. Lamarckian shortcut тАФ within-lifetime narrowing transmitted directly descendants, bypassing slow route requires mutations fix via selection many generations. contrast: Social learning significant effect stable environment copying output-layer weights successful neighbours propagates , certain . ╧Г prior width, policy weight, social copying transmit . Kin selection predicted retard canalization (energy subsidies buffer cost exploration, maintaining high ╧Г). effect negligible stable environment; kin altruism detectable effect ╧Г dynamics parameter values. prediction reversed: epigenetics produced largest effect across four experiments. title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: hook receives snapshot type (list(agents, grass, t, specs)), function signature identical across hooks. differs fires therefore state snapshot reflects. four closure variables (spatial_var, top_quartile, births_per_tick, plus drought pulse closure) now hold per-tick logs. can plot, summarise, save alongside standard output.
code: library(clade) clear_modules()  # pre_tick: freeze grass growth every 10 ticks to create a drought pulse register_module(   when = "pre_tick",   name = "drought_pulse",   fn   = function(env) {     if (env$t %% 10 == 0) env$grass[] <- 0     env   } )  # post_agents: record the spatial variance of post-move agent positions spatial_var <- numeric(0) register_module(   when = "post_agents",   name = "position_variance",   fn   = function(env) {     if (length(env$agents) > 1L) {       xs <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "x")       ys <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "y")       spatial_var <<- c(spatial_var, var(xs) + var(ys))     }     env   } )  # post_tick: accumulate top-quartile energy (standard logging) top_quartile <- numeric(0) register_module(   when = "post_tick",   name = "top_quartile_energy",   fn   = function(env) {     if (length(env$agents) > 0L) {       es <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "energy")       top_quartile <<- c(top_quartile, quantile(es, 0.75))     }     env   } )  # post_reproduce: record births actually realised this tick births_per_tick <- integer(0) register_module(   when = "post_reproduce",   name = "births",   fn   = function(env) {     # Note: newborns carry t_birth == env$t     n_new <- sum(vapply(env$agents,                         function(a) isTRUE(a$t_birth == env$t),                         logical(1L)))     births_per_tick <<- c(births_per_tick, n_new)     env   } )  specs <- default_specs(); specs$max_ticks <- 300L env <- run_alife(specs) # spatial_var, top_quartile, births_per_tick are now populated  clear_modules()

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: register_module(fn, , name) fn: function signature function(env) -> env. must return (possibly modified) snapshot тАФ return NULL something else, simulation error next hook. : one "pre_tick", "post_agents", "post_tick", "post_reproduce". Default "post_tick". name: label used list_modules() output. omitted, modules auto-named "module_1", "module_2", etc. list_modules() returns character vector "name ()" entries registration order. Multiple modules hook run order. clear_modules() empties registry. Calling runs essential donтАЩt want last runтАЩs modules applied next one.
code: register_module(fn, when = "post_tick", name = NULL) list_modules() clear_modules()

what: 4. Worked example тАФ top-quartile energy tracker
title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: common pattern: accumulate per-tick statistic closure variable inspect run. <<- makes pattern work: assigns top_quartile_energy vector enclosing scope, local copy. Without <<- module populate local variable discarded function returns. single common mistake custom modules.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  clear_modules()  top_quartile_energy <- numeric(0)  register_module(   when = "post_tick",   name = "top_quartile",   fn   = function(env) {     if (length(env$agents) > 0L) {       es <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "energy")       top_quartile_energy <<- c(top_quartile_energy, quantile(es, 0.75))     }     env   } )  specs <- default_specs(); specs$max_ticks <- 400L env <- run_alife(specs)  data <- get_run_data(env) data$ticks$top_quartile_energy <- c(NA, top_quartile_energy)[seq_len(nrow(data$ticks))]  ggplot(data$ticks, aes(t, top_quartile_energy)) +   geom_line() +   labs(title = "75th percentile of agent energy over time",        x = "Tick", y = "Q75(energy)") +   theme_minimal()  clear_modules()

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: module injects energy every agent exactly one tick тАФ modelling experimental food pulse: effect visible step change data$ticks$mean_energy tick 100. Compare paired control run clear_modules() quantify perturbationтАЩs downstream effect.
code: clear_modules()  register_module(   when = "post_tick",   name = "food_pulse",   fn   = function(env) {     if (env$t == 100L) {       for (i in seq_along(env$agents)) {         env$agents[[i]]$energy <- env$agents[[i]]$energy + 50       }     }     env   } )  specs <- default_specs(); specs$max_ticks <- 300L env  <- run_alife(specs) data <- get_run_data(env)  clear_modules()

what: 6. Worked example тАФ spatial spread logger
title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Record spatial variance agent positions tick тАФ measure tightly agents aggregate: "post_agents" right hook movement just happened deaths yet applied тАФ see positions live agents moves, biologically meaningful snapshot spatial dispersion.
code: library(clade)  clear_modules()  spatial_variance <- numeric(0)  register_module(   when = "post_agents",   name = "spatial_variance",   fn   = function(env) {     if (length(env$agents) > 1L) {       xs <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "x")       ys <- vapply(env$agents, `[[`, numeric(1L), "y")       spatial_variance <<- c(spatial_variance, var(xs) + var(ys))     }     env   } )  specs <- default_specs(); specs$max_ticks <- 400L env <- run_alife(specs)  # spatial_variance is a numeric vector indexed by tick plot(spatial_variance, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Spatial variance",      main = "Dispersion of agent positions")  clear_modules()

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: long-running analyses, wrap risky part tryCatch can log tick failed:
code: clear_modules()  error_log <- list()  register_module(   when = "post_tick",   name = "safe_pairwise",   fn   = function(env) {     result <- tryCatch({       # Anything risky тАФ matrix ops, I/O, external calls       pairwise_distance_matrix(env$agents)   # hypothetical helper     }, error = function(e) {       error_log[[length(error_log) + 1L]] <<- list(t = env$t, msg = conditionMessage(e))       NULL     })     # ... use result, possibly skipping this tick     env   } )

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: custom-module call crosses JuliaтЖФя╕ОR boundary per agent tick. marshalling cost scales population size agentтАЩs fields serialised R-readable form function runs. Concrete benchmark (documented inline R/modules.R:19-21): 200-agent ├Ч 500-tick run one post_tick custom module adds ~0.5тАУ1 s overhead compared run modules. 1,000 agents ├Ч 1,000 ticks, overhead climbs ~5тАУ10 s. Several registered modules approximately additive. Guidelines: Keep function body ~1 ms per call. Vectorise vapply, sapply, sum/mean/var rather loops agents. Avoid object creation inside function. Prefer <<- pre-allocated vector c(x, new_value) reallocates every tick. DonтАЩt file /O inside module тАФ batch writes run completes. need per-agent computation heavier microseconds per agent, тАЩre likely better modifying Julia kernel recompiling, rather using custom module. See kernel--biology chapters hot path. Hooks fire even modules registered тАФ registry lookup trivial JuliaтЖФя╕ОR boundary crossing skipped entirely list_modules() empty.

title: Getting started with clade
text: clade R package agent-based simulation evolution foraging ecology. Digital organisms carry heritable neural-network genomes (haploid diploid) one several brain types, evolve generations natural selection behaviour, life history, cognition. package designed evolutionary biologists want run controlled silico experiments without writing low-level simulation code. clade successor experimental alifeR package. alifeR embedded simulation hot path Rcpp, clade runs entire simulation Julia (via JuliaConnectoR). RтАУJulia boundary crossed per run_alife() call, per tick. 200 agents 1,000 ticks roughly 25├Ч faster Rcpp-based alifeR backend, makes runs 100,000 agents practical. Julia backend also enables MAP-Elites quality-diversity search derivative-free optimisation (CMA-ES), neither feasible scale Rcpp simulator. vignette orientation, reference. introduces workflow, parameter list, brain types, biological modules can toggled . code chunks shown evaluated, Julia backend available package check vignette build.

what: Simulation timescale: choosing the right spec preset
title: Getting started with clade
text: single important choice designing clade run many generations simulate. Fisher (1930) showed beneficial allele needs roughly 1/s1/s generations fix, ss selection coefficient. modest selection (sтЙИ0.05тАУ0.10s \approx 0.05\text{тАУ}0.10), 20тАУ100 generations. Weaker selection needs hundreds. default_specs(), generation time roughly max_age / 2 тЙИ 100 ticks plus maturation delay, effective generation time ~190 ticks. 500-tick run therefore ~2.6 generations тАФ short see evolutionary dynamics realistic selection strengths. single largest source тАЬweak effectтАЭ reports early clade audits. package ships three presets make trade-explicit: fast_specs() achieves short generation time lowering max_age 30 ticks min_repro_energy 60 (versus 200 120 default). MATLAB ancestor clade (Bulitko 2023) ran тАФ agents matured quickly turned fast, exactly gives selection enough time work. Rule thumb. phenomenon want see requires allele change (heritable trait evolution, genetic assimilation, cooperation signal elaboration), use fast_specs(). demographic ecological phenomenon unfolds within single generation (predator-prey cycles, disease spread, seasonal grass dynamics), default_specs() fine. See dev/docs/timescale-analysis.md biological reference table (E. coli elephant) full MATLAB-ancestor comparison motivated presets.
code: # Evolutionary scenario: use fast_specs evo <- fast_specs()               # max_ticks = 2000, ~66 generations evo$phenotypic_plasticity <- TRUE run_alife(evo)  # Within-generation ecology / LV demo: default_specs is appropriate ecol <- default_specs() ecol$max_ticks <- 500L ecol$n_predators_init <- 5L run_alife(ecol)

title: Getting started with clade
text: batch_alife() runs list specs across R worker processes. recommended way compare parameter regimes, since Julia process stays warm slice batch: systematic parameter exploration (CMA-ES, MAP-Elites, viability search), see parameter search guide.
code: specs_list <- lapply(c(0.05, 0.10, 0.20), function(gr) {   s <- default_specs()   s$grass_rate <- gr   s$max_ticks  <- 200L   s })  results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 3L)  # Compare mean genetic diversity across grass rates sapply(results, function(env) {   mean(get_run_data(env)$ticks$genetic_diversity, na.rm = TRUE) })

Subsequent calls reuse warm Julia session return results immediately.

previous_headings: death.jl тАФ when agents die and why
title: death.jl тАФ when agents die and why
text: says. Read four mortality parameters top: starvation_threshold тАФ die energy falls value (default 0). max_age тАФ die age reaches value (default 200). senescence_rate тАФ Gompertz mortality coefficient (default 0, meaning ). life_history тАФ "iteroparous" (default) "semelparous". latter triggers post-reproduction death. scavenging тАФ whether deposit carrion agent dies. Biology. demographic accounting. Every population biology model decide dies . clade exposes four orthogonal mortality processes user can combine: Starvation = ecological mortality (resource limitation). Age cap = hard maximum lifespan (simplification тАФ real animals donтАЩt fixed cap, just vanishing survival probability). Senescence = Gompertz mortality (Gompertz 1825) тАФ exponentially rising death probability age, standard demographic model vertebrate aging. Semelparous = post-reproductive death (salmon, annual plants, insects). default starvation age cap active. Senescence semelparous opt-. Audit findings. s-pace--life тЬЧ flagged always-max_age = 200 cap masks pace--life dynamics тАФ every agent dies cap metabolic-rate-driven differences accumulate. Tier 2 fix planned: scale max_age metabolic_rate fast-pace species shorter lifespans (R├йale 2010).
code: function kill_dead!(env::Environment)     specs     = env.specs     starv_th  = Float32(get(specs, "starvation_threshold", 0.0))     max_age   = Int(get(specs, "max_age", 200))     senes_r   = Float32(get(specs, "senescence_rate", 0.0))     semel     = get(specs, "life_history", "iteroparous") == "semelparous"     scav_on   = Bool(get(specs, "scavenging", false))

what: 3. The cause function тАФ applied in priority order
title: death.jl тАФ when agents die and why
text: says. Check death causes priority order. Return first one fires: Starvation тАФ energy threshold. Age cap тАФ age reached maximum. Gompertz senescence тАФ random draw age-dependent death probability. Semelparous тАФ agent reproduced tick life history set semelparous. none fires, return :alive. Biology. four causes encode four distinct biological processes:
code: function _death_cause(ag::Agent, starv_th::Float32, max_age::Int,                        senes_r::Float32, semel::Bool, rng)::Symbol     # 1. Starvation     ag.energy < starv_th && return :starvation      # 2. Age cap     ag.age >= max_age && return :age      # 3. Gompertz senescence     if senes_r > 0.0f0         # Scaled by heritable aging_rate: faster-aging genotypes die sooner         eff_r = senes_r * ag.aging_rate         p_die = Float32(1.0 - exp(-Float64(eff_r) * exp(Float64(eff_r) * Float64(ag.age))))         rand(rng) < p_die && return :senescence     end      # 4. Semelparous     semel && ag.reproduced && return :semelparous      :alive end

text: says. agent dies, record seven fields: id, tick, age death, energy death, cause, body size, lifetime offspring count. Biology. life-table data supports demographic analyses downstream: Survival curves тАФ distribution age--death cause. Lifetime reproductive success тАФ num_offspring per individual. code: function _log_death!(env::Environment, ag::Agent, cause::Symbol)     push!(env.deaths["id"],        Int(ag.id))     push!(env.deaths["t"],         Int(env.t))     push!(env.deaths["age"],       Int(ag.age))     push!(env.deaths["energy"],    Float64(ag.energy))     push!(env.deaths["cause"],     string(cause))     push!(env.deaths["body_size"], Float64(ag.body_size))     push!(env.deaths["num_offspring"], Int(ag.num_offspring)) end

text: says. haploid parent: copy single strand weights, mutate , return. diploid parent: n_chromosomes chunks weights: Flip coin decide strand (maternal paternal) start тАФ independent assortment (MendelтАЩs second law). Sample Poisson number crossover positions. Walk chromosome, switching strands crossover. chromosomes assembled, mutate whole gamete Gaussian noise. Biology. textbook diploid meiosis (Charlesworth & Charlesworth 2010, Chapter 5). three classical steps: Independent assortment тАФ chromosome chooses parent strand independently. Mendel discovered pea plants (second law). Recombination / crossover тАФ within chromosome, gamete alternates two parental strands random crossover points. shuffles linked genes within generation. Poisson model rate crossover_rate standard continuous-time approximation. Mutation тАФ recombination, Gaussian noise added every weight. generates new variation natural selection acts . biologically realistic part kernel clade-specific improvement alifeR (used flat-weight crossover chromosome structure). Audit findings. s-pop-genetics тЬЕ тАФ heritability proxy (lag-1 autocorrelation mean body_size) 0.992, exactly working Mendelian inheritance system produce. Variants worth considering. Sex chromosomes тАФ currently sex-linked inheritance. Adding тАЬsex chromosomeтАЭ (one copy males, two females, hemizygous expression) let clade test sex-linked trait scenarios. Variable crossover rate тАФ currently constant per simulation. Real species variable recombination rates; heritable crossover_rate trait small extension. Inversions тАФ large structural rearrangements suppress recombination locally. Important many real-species scenarios (e.g., supergenes). implemented.
code: function meiosis(parent::DiploidGenome, specs, rng)::Vector{Float32}     if is_haploid(parent)         return _mutate_weights(copy(parent.maternal_weights), specs, rng)     end      n       = length(parent.maternal_weights)     gamete  = Vector{Float32}(undef, n)     nc      = Int(parent.n_chromosomes)     seg_len = div(n, nc)      ╬╗ = Float64(get(specs, "crossover_rate", 1.0))      for c in 1:nc         lo = (c - 1) * seg_len + 1         hi = c == nc ? n : c * seg_len          # Choose starting strand by independent assortment         using_mat = rand(rng) < 0.5          # Sample crossover positions (Poisson number)         k = _rpois(rng, ╬╗)         xpts = sort!(rand(rng, lo:hi, k))          pos = lo         for xp in xpts             src = using_mat ? parent.maternal_weights : parent.paternal_weights             gamete[pos:xp] .= src[pos:xp]             pos = xp + 1             using_mat = !using_mat         end         # Fill remainder of segment         src = using_mat ? parent.maternal_weights : parent.paternal_weights         gamete[pos:hi] .= src[pos:hi]     end      _mutate_weights(gamete, specs, rng) end

code: function meiosis_traits(parent, specs, rng)::Vector{Float32}     if is_haploid(parent)         return _mutate_traits(copy(parent.maternal_traits), specs, rng)     end      # Each trait inherits from a randomly chosen parent allele     gamete = Vector{Float32}(undef, N_SCALAR_TRAITS)     for i in 1:N_SCALAR_TRAITS         gamete[i] = rand(rng) < 0.5 ?                     parent.maternal_traits[i] : parent.paternal_traits[i]     end     _mutate_traits(gamete, specs, rng) end

text: says. Convert diploid genome (two strands) single phenotype (one set weights actually used brain). Three modes: Haploid тАФ phenotype just maternal strand (choice needed). Dominant тАФ weight, randomly pick maternal paternal. Implements random-allele-expression interpretation dominance. Additive (default) тАФ phenotype average maternal paternal. Implements codominant / additive model standard quantitative genetics. Biology. Default "additive" quantitative-genetics standard traits many small contributing loci (Falconer & Mackay 1996). Brain weights explicitly quantitative тАФ additive right default. code: function express_weights(g::DiploidGenome, dominance_model::String, rng)     if is_haploid(g) || dominance_model == "dominant"         if is_haploid(g)             return copy(g.maternal_weights)         end         # Dominant: random allele per weight         n = length(g.maternal_weights)         result = Vector{Float32}(undef, n)         for i in 1:n             result[i] = rand(rng) < 0.5 ?                         g.maternal_weights[i] : g.paternal_weights[i]         end         return result     end     # Additive (default): midpoint of maternal and paternal     return (g.maternal_weights .+ g.paternal_weights) ./ 2.0f0 end

code: function _mutate_weights(w::Vector{Float32}, specs, rng)     sd = Float32(get(specs, "mutation_sd", 0.1))     sd > 0.0f0 || return w     @inbounds for i in eachindex(w)         w[i] += sd * randn(rng)     end     w end

code: function genome_distance(g1::DiploidGenome, g2::DiploidGenome,                           dominance_model::String = "additive")::Float32     p1 = express_weights(g1, dominance_model, default_rng())     p2 = express_weights(g2, dominance_model, default_rng())     n  = max(length(p1), length(p2))     n == 0 && return 0.0f0     sqrt(sum(abs2, p1 .- p2)) / sqrt(Float32(n)) end

text: says. Loop every agent. Skip dead, already reproduced tick, young, insufficient energy. Stop entirely population cap reached. Allee threshold set, also skip agents without enough neighbours. Biology. Four biological gates: Alive. Dead agents donтАЩt reproduce. already reproduced tick. agent reproduces per tick. (semelparous species reproduced flag used death.jl kill parent next tick.) Age threshold. min_repro_age, reproduction. Default 0 (clade) тАФ age can reproduce тАФ differs ancestors (MATLAB alifeR required minimum age). simplification can reversed user. Real animals universally juvenile period. Energy threshold. Must least effective_repro_threshold energy. effective_ prefix means plasticity-adjusted: phenotypic_plasticity = TRUE, threshold can vary per agent. Allee effect (optional, default). critical neighbour density, reproduction stops. Models species depend group cohesion breeding (Allee 1931). Audit findings. s-life-history audit тЬЕ verified eligibility logic. Semelparous iteroparous strategies produce distinct demographic signatures (mean age 13 vs 102, births 4.2 vs 0.9 per tick). reproduced flag interaction death.jlтАЩs semelparous trigger wired correctly. Variants worth considering. Reproductive senescence тАФ older agents reproduce reduced probability. Currently can implemented via senescence_rate parameter death.jl, causes death rather fecundity reduction. Condition-dependent threshold тАФ threshold depend agentтАЩs body size, social rank, etc.
code: for ag in env.agents     ag.alive              || continue     ag.reproduced         && continue     ag.age < min_repro_age && continue     ag.energy < effective_repro_threshold(ag, env) && continue     length(env.agents) + length(new_agents) >= max_ag && break      # Allee effect: count neighbours     if allee_th > 0         n_nbrs = _count_neighbours(ag, env)         n_nbrs < allee_th && continue     end

text: says. Determine clutch size reproduction event. clutch_size_evolution , sample truncated normal (mean = init_mean, sd = mutation_sd, clamped [clutch_size_min, clutch_size_max]). Otherwise use fixed max_clutch_size. Update bookkeeping counters. Biology. Clutch size one -studied life-history traits (Lack 1947, Ibis 89:302-352). trade-: many small offspring vs large ones. Lack proposed optimal clutch size one maximises raised offspring (just born). cladeтАЩs implementation lets clutch size evolve heritable trait, sampled per parent per reproduction event. Truncated-normal sampling introduces stochastic variation тАФ biologically reasonable, since real clutch sizes vary among individuals even identical genotypes. Audit findings. s-clutch-size тЬЕ recovered bell-shaped r/K response: clutch rises grass_rate (r-arm) 0.15, falls population hits cap density-dependent K-selection takes . Clean recovery MacArthur-Wilson r/K theory population level. Variants worth considering. Iteroparous birth distribution тАФ currently every agent gives birth full clutch one tick, waits reproduced flag clear next tick. Real iteroparous species often spread births across days/weeks within season. Maternal effects тАФ clutch size real species influenced maternal condition, age, even prior reproductive history. Currently genome-encoded clutch trait matters.

text: says. offspring clutch: Find mate (set mate nothing asexual reproduction). Compute cost_paid тАФ either fixed repro_cost repro_cost_fraction * parent_energy, depending mode. Deduct parentтАЩs energy. mate present, deduct half mate . Compute offspringтАЩs starting energy тАФ either fixed offspring_energy offspring_energy_fraction * cost_paid, per second mode flag. Biology. parental investment trade-тАФ mathematical core life-history theory. Smith & Fretwell (1974) showed optimal allocation parental resources depends marginal fitness gain per unit invested single offspring. practice means: Parents better condition can invest total per-offspring. unit investment yields diminishing returns offspring fitness тАФ single super-rich offspring isnтАЩt necessarily fit two moderately-rich ones. 0.4.0 default ("proportional") implements directly: cost_paid = 0.5 * parent.energy (default repro_cost_fraction = 0.5) offspring_energy = 0.25 * cost_paid (default offspring_energy_fraction = 0.25) 75% cost тАЬlost developmentтАЭ тАФ eggshells, gestation inefficiency, placental drain, etc. 25-40% conversion efficiency documented many vertebrates. mate paying half cost models biparental investment тАФ stable many vertebrates birds. Asexual single-parent species get full cost. Audit findings. Pre-0.4.0, s-parental-investment audit ЁЯЯа female_investment didnтАЩt couple outcomes. 0.4.0 proportional cost, тАФ cost_paid chain off_energy_actual. Re-audit pending. Variants worth considering. Asymmetric mate cost тАФ currently mate pays exactly half. species different investments (e.g., brood-parasitic birds: mate pays 0%; eusocial workers: queen pays 100%). Quality-vs-quantity selection тАФ fixed cost_paid, varying offspring_energy_fraction upward produces fewer high-quality offspring (since cost > offspring energy means тАЬlost developmentтАЭ inefficiency rises). testable Smith-Fretwell knob.
code: for _ in 1:clutch     length(env.agents) + length(new_agents) >= max_ag && break      # Find mate (or reproduce asexually)     mate = _find_mate(ag, env)      # 0.4.0: parental cost     #   "fixed"        тАФ deduct constant `repro_cost` (legacy)     #   "proportional" тАФ deduct `repro_cost_fraction * parent.energy`     #                    (Smith & Fretwell 1974; default in 0.4.0)     cost_paid = if repro_cost_mode == "proportional"         repro_cost_frac * ag.energy     else         repro_cost     end     ag.energy -= cost_paid     mate !== nothing && (mate.energy -= cost_paid * 0.5f0)      # 0.4.0: offspring birth energy     #   "fixed"        тАФ every newborn starts with `offspring_energy`     #                    (legacy; ignores parent condition)     #   "proportional" тАФ newborn starts with `offspring_energy_fraction     #                    * cost_paid` per Smith-Fretwell quality-quantity     #                    (default in 0.4.0)     off_energy_actual = if off_energy_mode == "proportional"         off_energy_frac * cost_paid     else         off_energy_base     end

text: says. function tick_agents! takes whole environment argument. Inside, pulls user-set parameters (specs), grid dimensions, whether grid toroidal (.e.┬аworld wrap around edges, like old Asteroids game, walls). Biology. grid world. Toroidal means edges тАФ useful modelling habitats continuous agentтАЩs perspective (patch forest large enough individual rarely encounters boundary). Bounded (non-toroidal) grids matter invasion-front dynamics like ShineтАЩs spatial sorting (s-dispersal-ifd) front real ecological feature. Variants worth considering. Real habitats rarely either perfectly toroidal perfectly bounded тАФ edges permeability gradients (forest fading pasture). clade currently supports two extremes. тАЬsoft edgeтАЭ mode (probability re-entry decreasing distance grid centre) realistically model habitat fragmentation.

what: 2. Per-tick costs and gains тАФ the energy economy parameters
title: tick.jl тАФ one tick in the life of an agent
text: says. Read user-set energetic constants top function rather looking every iteration agent loop: move_cost = 1.0 тАФ energy spent moving one cell. idle_cost = 0.5 тАФ energy spent staying place. (Half movement cost, zero тАФ even resting animal burns energy.) eat_gain = 5.0 тАФ energy gained per unit grass consumed. max_bite = 2.0 тАФ maximum grass agent can eat one tick one cell. Added 0.4.0, see audit findings . three brain-energy parameters control expensive thinking . Biology. four parameters carve model basal metabolic rate resource intake. numbers matter less ratios тАФ matters : Moving costs resting (, real animals). Eating yields energy moving costs (otherwise foraging losing strategy). maximum per-tick intake bounded (real animals handling time gut capacity тАФ canтАЩt eat infinite food one bush one moment). max_bite parameter handling time constraint, well-known HollingтАЩs disc equation (Holling 1959, Can. Entomol. 91:385-398). Without , forager finds rich patch can extract entire content instantly тАФ manifest unrealism. Audit findings. max_bite parameter missing clade 0.3.x тАФ agents stripped cell entirely tick. fidelity audit flagged one ten kernel rules diverged ancestor implementations (MATLAB Bulitko 2023; alifeR R port). Restored 0.4.0. See dev/docs/kernel-0.4.0.md full reasoning. Variants worth considering. Real animalsтАЩ bite size scales body mass (larger animals take bigger bites). future kernel change make max_bite proportional agentтАЩs body_size trait, coupling two existing modules. 0.4.0 backlog Tier 2 improvement.

title: tick.jl тАФ one tick in the life of an agent
text: says. Pass input vector agentтАЩs brain (evolved neural network), getting back vector five тАЬlogitsтАЭ тАФ one possible action (north, east, south, west, idle). Pick action highest logit. Increment two counters tracking many total decisions many greedy decisions agent made. Biology. behavioural decision-making, simplified core: gather information, run brain, choose action. brain heritable structure (genome encodes networkтАЩs weights) foraging strategy evolves across generations. тАЬgreedyтАЭ choice (always pick highest-scoring action) one two exploration schemes: ANN, CTRNN, GRN brains: greedy = deterministic policy, behaviour fully determined current input. BNN brain (default): greedy randomised BNN samples weights posterior distribution forward pass. Two presentations input can yield two different actions sigma > 0. Thompson sampling, mathematically equivalent soft exploration reinforcement-learning theory. Audit findings. BNNтАЩs per-tick weight resampling (s-baldwin, s-rl, s-social-learning audits) dilutes within-lifetime learning тАФ copied RL-trained weights get drowned next tickтАЩs random sample. Tier 5 backlog item: make BNN sample per significant decision interval instead every tick. Variants worth considering. animals (especially insects) behave ways closer fixed action patterns flexible decision-making. тАЬ-brain random walkтАЭ mode (brain_type = "random", already implemented) provides control. ecological realism, animals intermediate flexibility тАФ habitual behaviour interspersed situational responses. cladeтАЩs BNN captures sigma tuned.
code: logits = forward(ag.brain, inp) action = argmax(logits)           # greedy; BNN exploration via sigma ag.num_choices        += Int32(1) ag.num_greedy_choices += Int32(1)

what: 6. Move тАФ the energy cost of action
title: tick.jl тАФ one tick in the life of an agent
text: says. Record agentтАЩs energy tickтАЩs actions (energy_last_tick, used RL module active). Update position based chosen action. North decreases row index; south increases ; east/west change column. Idle leaves position alone. action deducts energy cost, scaled agentтАЩs metabolic rate (heritable trait тАФ agents higher metabolic_rate pay action). Movement costs resting. Biology. mechanical-cost--living rule. Energy universal currency: Movement metabolically expensive (locomotion, oxygen demand, muscular work). Resting still costs something (basal metabolic rate тАФ heart, brain, organs consume energy even rest). cost scales agentтАЩs intrinsic metabolic rate (Kleiber 1932, Hilgardia 6:315-353; Brown et al.┬а2004, Ecology 85:1771- 1789). Action 5 (idle) costs less movement zero тАФ biologically correct (basal vs active metabolism differ nonzero). MATLAB ancestor charged zero idle; alifeR port charged full movement cost (documented bug). cladeтАЩs split move_cost idle_cost realistic either ancestor. Audit findings. specific issues тАФ rule sound matches biology. metabolic_rate heritable trait wired correctly; тАЩs broken downstream (always-age cap overrides pace--life signals тАФ see audit s-pace--life). Variants worth considering. Real movement costs scale body mass (larger animals pay per metre per metre per gram less тАФ KleiberтАЩs law). future change make move_cost scale body_size. Different terrain types carry different movement costs (mud slower open ground). Currently cost uniform across grid. fine cladeтАЩs grass-grid abstraction matter grid grew complex. current N/E/S/W movement rook-style (diagonal). Real animals typically use king-style (8 directions) movement. Adding diagonals small kernel change biological payoff.
code: ag.energy_last_tick = ag.energy x, y = Int(ag.x), Int(ag.y) if action == 1      # N     x = wrap_or_clamp(x - 1, rows, toroidal)     ag.energy -= move_cost * ag.metabolic_rate elseif action == 2  # E     y = wrap_or_clamp(y + 1, cols, toroidal)     ag.energy -= move_cost * ag.metabolic_rate elseif action == 3  # S     x = wrap_or_clamp(x + 1, rows, toroidal)     ag.energy -= move_cost * ag.metabolic_rate elseif action == 4  # W     y = wrap_or_clamp(y - 1, cols, toroidal)     ag.energy -= move_cost * ag.metabolic_rate else                # idle     ag.energy -= idle_cost * ag.metabolic_rate end ag.x = Int32(x) ag.y = Int32(y)

what: 7. Eat тАФ the resource intake rule (0.4.0 change)
title: tick.jl тАФ one tick in the life of an agent
text: says. тАЩs grass agentтАЩs current cell, take either max_bite worth, whichever smaller. Convert bite energy via eat_gain. Remove eaten amount cell. call eat_layered! (-op unless complex-landscape module enabled тАФ , agents can also eat shrub canopy layers). Finally, cap agentтАЩs energy energy_max (default 200) тАФ agents canтАЩt accumulate infinite reserves. Biology. foraging тАФ central process trophic ecology. Three biological constraints encoded: Eat stand. agent gets resources current cell. тАЩs reaching, caching, sharing (modules add ). matches grazing herbivores well; less well predator-prey systems chase--handle norm. Handling time (max_bite). agent can process much per tick. HollingтАЩs Type II functional response simplest form (Holling 1959). Storage capacity (energy_max). Real organisms finite storage тАФ fat reserves, glycogen pools, gut volume. full, excess intake wasted. 0.3.x kernel single line eating: тАЬstrip cell entirely one tick.тАЭ ancestors enforced max_bite; clade outlier. Audit (Tier 1, 0.4.0) flagged restored handling time. Audit findings. 0.3.x strip--cell rule produced two artefacts: Per-tick energy windfalls. Finding rich cell yield eat_gain * grass_max = 25 energy one tick тАФ distorting reproductive timing post-windfall agent immediately crossed min_repro_energy. Suppressed density-dependence. cell feed one agent per visit; second visitors found empty. made shared-resource scenarios (cooperation, kin altruism) harder test. max_bite = 2, maximum windfall 10 energy rich cells sustain multiple grazings. Variants worth considering. Bite size scaling body size (mentioned earlier). Different resource types (fruit, leaves, insects) different eat_gain max_bite. complex-landscape module start; adding nutritional dimensions extend . Group foraging: multiple agents cell cooperate extract (compete extract less). Currently agent eats independently turn (order side effect ag env.agents loop).
code: # 0.4.0: handling-time-limited intake. An agent extracts at most # `max_bite` units of grass from the cell per tick. Multiple ticks # (or multiple agents on consecutive ticks) are needed to deplete # a rich cell. Restores alifeR / MATLAB-Bulitko `maxbite` semantics. if env.grass[x, y] > 0.0f0     bite             = min(env.grass[x, y], max_bite)     ag.energy       += eat_gain * bite     env.grass[x, y] -= bite end eat_layered!(ag, env)   # complex landscape: shrub/canopy supplements ag.energy = min(ag.energy, Float32(get(specs, "energy_max", 200.0))) ag.energy += eat_gain * env.grass[x, y] env.grass[x, y] = 0.0f0

what: 8. Brain energy cost тАФ thinking is expensive
title: tick.jl тАФ one tick in the life of an agent
text: helper function: says. eating, deduct additional energy cost running brain. cost depends mode active: "none" тАФ free thinking. cost. (Useful control runs.) "size" тАФ cost proportional brain size. bigger brain costs maintain, regardless active . "activity" (default) тАФ cost = size cost + activity cost. activity component scales strongly brain firing (average absolute logit value). "prediction_error" тАФ currently placeholder, falls back size cost. Biology. Brains expensive organs. Human brains use roughly 20% total energy budget despite 2% body mass (Aiello & Wheeler 1995, Curr. Anthropol. 36:199-221). тАЬexpensive brain hypothesisтАЭ standard framework: large brains require sustained high-quality energy input maintain, constrains brain evolution. cladeтАЩs three modes encode three different theories brain cost: "size" corresponds expensive-tissue hypothesis тАФ larger brains cost maintain (basal metabolic load). "activity" corresponds attention cost (Yaeger 1994, PolyWorld) тАФ thinking harder costs . default adds within-lifetime selection pressure energy-efficient policies. "prediction_error" reserved information-theoretic cost (KL divergence prior posterior) isnтАЩt implemented. MATLAB ancestor charged sum(|weights|) * annPowerCoefficient тАФ size-based weight magnitude proxy. alifeR didnтАЩt charge brain cost . cladeтАЩs тАЬactivityтАЭ mode biologically motivated three тАФ novel. Audit findings. s-brain-size scenario tests parental- provisioning hypothesis: care needed bootstrap large brains newborns canтАЩt yet forage well. Audit found effect directionally correct tiny default parameters тАФ ╬Ф = 0.009 units care -care. Tier 2 backlog: rebalance brain_energy_base brain_energy_activity cost consequential. Variants worth considering. Brain cost arguably scale super-linearly brain size (larger brains disproportionate maintenance costs blood supply, cooling, ). Current scaling linear. Brain cost might also depend agent тАФ high cognitive load tasks (hunting, navigating) cost habitual ones (grazing). Currently task-dependence; activity mode closest approximation. Newborns juveniles might building costs addition running costs. Currently running cost modelled; parental-care module covers development costs separately.
code: ag.energy -= _brain_energy_cost(ag.brain, logits, e_mode, e_base, e_act) function _brain_energy_cost(brain::AbstractBrain,                               logits::Vector{Float32},                               mode::String,                               base::Float32,                               act_scale::Float32)::Float32     mode == "none" && return 0.0f0     size_cost = base * Float32(brain_size(brain))     mode == "size" && return size_cost     if mode == "activity"         act_cost = act_scale * sum(abs, logits) / Float32(length(logits))         return size_cost + act_cost     end     # prediction_error тАФ use size cost as fallback (Phase 3 will implement KL)     size_cost end

previous_headings: Neural architecture
title: Parameter Reference
text: six brain types differ expressive power computational cost: bnn (Bayesian neural network): default; learns distribution weights. ann: standard multilayer perceptron. ctrnn: continuous-time recurrent network; suitable temporally extended tasks. grn: gene regulatory network topology; sparse, biologically motivated. transformer: self-attention architecture; highest capacity, slowest. synthesis: symbolic rule extraction evolved weights.

title: Parameter Reference
text: Enable coevolving_parasites = TRUE either signal_dims > 0 (continuous mode) n_parasite_loci > 0 (discrete mode). Continuous mode (0.5.0) uses Euclidean distance signal vector tracks population centroid. Sex offspring cluster closer centroid asex clones, sex exposed тАФ reproduce HamiltonтАЩs canonical Red Queen. Discrete mode (0.5.1) uses Hamming distance binary-allele haplotype Mendelian inheritance (locus inherited independently either parent). Recombination produces novel haplotype combinations escape current parasite genotype. canonical Hamilton mechanism first regime clade shows sex > asex.

title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: clade agent-based simulation. Interesting science usually requires varying parameter, varying several parameters, evolutionary search joint parameter space тАФ sufficient scale result distribution informative, just handful runs. article practical guide running tens thousands millions run_alife() scenarios across CPU cores without running memory, deadlocking Julia backend, losing state job dies overnight. Every helper introduces exported clade R package 0.5.6. four problems article solves: Generating parameter combinations тАФ grids, random draws, user-supplied distributions. Running parallel тАФ across R worker processes, Julia session (non-deadlocking path). Streaming results disk тАФ 1 M runs doesnтАЩt mean 1 M copies env$progress RAM. Resuming killed job тАФ crash run 800 k 1 M isnтАЩt fatal.

what: Important fix: parallelism at n_cores > 1 was silently broken
title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: 0.5.6, batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = N > 1) used parallel::mclapply, forks R process. forked R children inherited parentтАЩs JuliaConnectoR socket couldnтАЩt safely multiplex тАФ practice, workers Julia server went idle batch hung forever. 0.5.6 fixes swapping parallel::makeCluster("PSOCK") тАФ separate R processes, Julia session, shared socket. running parallel sweeps getting mysterious hangs single-core speeds, ; update 0.5.6+ parallelise. Cost know : PSOCK worker boots Julia session first call, ~60 s compile hit. small batches (< 20 scenarios) serial faster; 50+ scenarios parallel speedup near-linear. See dev/docs/parallelism-audit.md full post-mortem.

what: stream_specs_to_csv() тАФ stream results to disk
title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: Appropriate full result objects fit RAM (10 k 1 M runs) want survive crash: Flow per run: call run_alife(), call summary_fn (default writes varied parameters + n_final + mean_energy_final + diversity_final + viability), append one CSV row, discard env object. 1 M runs stays hundred MB RAM. Resumability: resume = TRUE checks CSV disk existing run_id values skips . run named specs_list gets list-name run_id; otherwise gets run_000001, run_000002, etc. crashed--restarted overnight job just runs stream_specs_to_csv(...) out_path picks left . Custom summary function:
code: stream_specs_to_csv(   specs_list,   out_path   = "/data/sweeps/grass_mutation_sweep.csv",   n_cores    = 50L,   resume     = TRUE   # default )  # Reads back as a regular CSV tbl <- read.csv("/data/sweeps/grass_mutation_sweep.csv") stream_specs_to_csv(specs_list, "/data/custom.csv", n_cores = 50L,   summary_fn = function(env, specs) {     d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks     list(       cycling_amplitude = diff(range(d$n_agents)),       final_mean_age    = tail(d$mean_age, 1L),       n_starvations    = sum(d$n_starvations)     )   })

title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: search-driven parameter exploration (gradient-free optimisation quality-diversity search) use built-adaptive algorithms: algorithms use PSOCK path internally 0.5.6+. Checkpoint/resume long MAP-Elites runs (new 0.5.6): pass checkpoint_path current archive + history + iteration index saved RDS every checkpoint_every iterations (end). process dies, re-running call picks saved iteration: checkpoint RDS also useful mid-run exploratory analysis тАФ can readRDS() file another R session plot current archive without interrupting search.
code: # CMA-ES: minimise a user-defined loss over the parameter space res_cma <- search_cmaes(   base_specs = fast_specs(),   params     = list(grass_rate = c(0.05, 0.45),                     mutation_sd = c(0.02, 0.2)),   fitness_fn = function(env) -tail(env$progress$n_agents, 1L),   n_gens     = 20L,   pop_size   = 20L,   n_cores    = 20L )  # MAP-Elites: quality-diversity search res_me <- search_map_elites(   base_specs = fast_specs(),   params     = list(grass_rate = c(0.05, 0.45),                     predator_energy_gain = c(10, 60)),   descriptors = c("grass_rate", "predator_energy_gain"),   fitness_fn = function(env) tail(env$progress$mean_energy, 1L),   archive_res = c(10L, 10L),   n_evals    = 1000L,   n_cores    = 20L ) res_me <- search_map_elites(   base_specs       = fast_specs(),   archive_dims     = list(mean_energy = seq(40, 120, length.out = 10L),                           genetic_diversity = seq(0.1, 1, length.out = 10L)),   n_iterations     = 100000L,   mutation_sd      = 0.2,   n_cores          = 20L,   checkpoint_path  = "/scratch/me_archive.rds",   checkpoint_every = 500L ) # If this dies at iter 73,421, just run the same call again тАФ it # resumes at 73,421 + 1 and runs to 100,000.

what: How big can I go on one machine?
title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: Rough numbers (assumes fast_specs() 500-tick probe тЙИ 5 s/run): n_cores cap machine 200 (see CLAUDE.md). Beyond ~1 M runs per machine-day want cluster.

what: How big can I go on a cluster?
title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: submit_sweep_slurm() (new 0.5.6) writes SLURM array-job template . submit тАФ run sbatch тАФ handles mechanical parts: splitting specs_list chunks, writing per-task R script reads SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID processes right slice, pointing every task resume-safe CSV. array task: Reads task index SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID. Loads slice specs_list shared RDS. Runs stream_specs_to_csv() slice n_cores_per_task cores resume = TRUE. Resume-safe CSV + chunked specs means preempted stalled tasks can re-run without re-processing completed specs. 100 k-run sweep split 200 ways, task processes 500 specs тАФ 5 s/run ├Ч 8 cores = ~5 min per task everything goes right, even 10-minute SLURM slot per task re-queue tolerant. currently wired: future.batchtools (alternative backend abstraction) clustermq adapter. Open issue тАЩd rather use .
code: specs_list <- sample_specs(fast_specs(), n = 100000L,                            grass_rate  = list(0.05, 0.45),                            mutation_sd = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2))  submit_sweep_slurm(   specs_list,   out_path         = "/shared/sweeps/big_sweep.csv",   script_path      = "/shared/sweeps/submit.sh",   rds_path         = "/shared/sweeps/specs.rds",   n_array_tasks    = 200L,   n_cores_per_task = 8L,   time             = "12:00:00",   mem              = "16G" ) # prints:  sbatch /shared/sweeps/submit.sh

title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: run_alife() now attaches env$viability report every run warn()s population crashed. streaming thousands runs, crashed runs still produce summary row (viability = "crashed") тАФ can filter re-run afterwards:
code: tbl <- read.csv("/data/sweeps/big_sweep.csv") table(tbl$viability) #>  viable  weak  crashed #>   78341 15822     5837  # Re-run only the crashed cases at a tamer parameter regime: to_rerun <- as.character(tbl$run_id[tbl$viability == "crashed"]) retry_specs <- specs_list[to_rerun] for (i in seq_along(retry_specs))   retry_specs[[i]]$grass_rate <- retry_specs[[i]]$grass_rate * 1.3 stream_specs_to_csv(retry_specs, "/data/sweeps/big_sweep_retry.csv",                     n_cores = 50L, resume = TRUE)

title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)
text: dev/benchmarks/brain_comparison.R script repo compact, reproducible end--end use tooling. runs 5 brain types ├Ч 5 seeds = 25 independent clade simulations parallel produces faceted comparison figure. full code: See vignette("s-brain-comparison") full analysis + saved figure. Takeaways map cleanly canonical life-history theory: BNN тЖТ density strategy (196 agents ├Ч 125 energy) GRN тЖТ quality strategy (56 agents ├Ч 170 energy) ANN / CTRNN intermediate; random fragile
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2); library(patchwork)  BRAIN_TYPES <- c("bnn", "ann", "ctrnn", "grn", "random") SEEDS       <- c(1L, 7L, 13L, 19L, 25L)  base <- default_specs() base$grid_rows     <- 30L; base$grid_cols <- 30L base$n_agents_init <- 80L; base$max_agents <- 400L base$grass_rate    <- 0.15 base$max_ticks     <- 500L  specs_list <- grid_specs(base,                          brain_type  = BRAIN_TYPES,                          random_seed = SEEDS) # 25 PSOCK workers, each with its own Julia; Julia compile # happens in parallel so wall clock is ~60 s startup + run time. results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = length(specs_list))  # Pull out summary + full trajectories tbl <- summarize_batch(results, specs_list,                        param_names = c("brain_type", "random_seed")) traj <- do.call(rbind, Map(function(env, specs) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   d$brain_type <- specs$brain_type   d$seed       <- specs$random_seed   d[, c("t", "n_agents", "mean_energy", "brain_type", "seed")] }, results, specs_list))

title: Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: 15 TRAIT_* scalar traits live genome express per individual. silent (fixed biologically neutral default) unless _evolution flag TRUE. can search either init mean (ask population starts) _mutation_sd (ask fast drifts), . _evolution flag FALSE, init-mean mutation-SD ignored. trait clamped biologically neutral value (usually 1.0 multipliers, 0.0 zero-centred propensities). catches beginners: setting body_size_init_mean = 2.0 body_size_evolution = FALSE effect. fidelity audit pace--life trace cautionary example exact pitfall.

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: Four architecture knobs, searchable: brain_type categorical тАФ search looping conditions rather passing CMA-ES. bnn_sigma_source also categorical; two non-default modes introduced 0.4.0 specifically make Baldwin Effect testable (see kernel-0.4.0.md).

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: max_age_scales_with_metabolism = TRUE lets pace--life scenarios show predicted fast-pace-short-life pattern; otherwise every agent dies base cap regardless metabolic rate.

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: 0.4.0 rewrote reproduction-cost accounting. legacy fixed mode still available reproducing pre-0.4.0 results, biological default now proportional (Smith & Fretwell 1974). female_investment coupling new 0.4.0 тАФ -op . See s-parental-investment ╧Б = тИТ1.00 dose-response now produces.

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: Kin altruism textbook case clean parameter search: 9-cell B ├Ч C grid fixed r_min = 0.25 recovers HamiltonтАЩs rule Spearman ╧Б = 0.97, similar grid cooperation multiplier recovers NowakтАУMay ╧Б = 1.00. See s-kin s-cooperation.

what: Worked example: random search over agent parameters
title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: cheapest way explore тАФ 30 simulations random points inside hyper-box specify. Use first locate productive regions committing expensive algorithm. result data frame sorted descending score. row one simulation; score mean genetic_diversity across ticks. Retrieve full specs best configuration:
code: library(clade)  result <- search_random(   specs_base    = default_specs(),   search_params = list(     mutation_sd             = c(0.01, 0.5),     crossover_rate          = c(0.0, 2.0),     body_size_mutation_sd   = c(0.01, 0.2)   ),   n_samples = 30L,   objective = "genetic_diversity" )  head(result) best_specs <- attr(result, "specs_list")[[1]] cat("Best mutation_sd:", best_specs$mutation_sd, "\n") cat("Best crossover_rate:", best_specs$crossover_rate, "\n")

what: Worked example: CMA-ES on brain hyperparameters
title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: want single best brain configuration search space continuous (BNN prior width, learning rate, body-size trait), CMA-ES gives good answer ~20 iterations ├Ч popsize 10 = 200 runs. Use learning_rate_evolution = TRUE + rl_mode = "actor_critic" combination test Baldwin Effect directly тАФ exactly setup auto-calibration harness dev/audit/calibration/ uses.
code: result <- search_cmaes(   specs_base = default_specs(),   params     = c("bnn_sigma_init",                  "learning_rate_init_mean",                  "body_size_mutation_sd"),   objective  = "genetic_diversity",   n_iterations = 20L,   popsize      = 10L )  cat("Best diversity:", result$score, "\n") print(result$specs[c("bnn_sigma_init",                      "learning_rate_init_mean",                      "body_size_mutation_sd")])

title: Parameter search тАФ agent-level parameters
text: domain-specific tune_iffolk() wrapper pre-configures relevant agent-level parameters (transfer rate, parliament cost, helper tendency evolution) uses objective_iffolk(), fits linear regression mean_helper_tendency time rewards upward trends plus per-agent transfer bonus. far sensitive simply maximising final helper tendency: Pair search_viability() first тАФ see ps-environment-parameters.html#viability-mapping тАФ extreme parameter values IFfolk population goes extinct CMA-ES signal follow.
code: tuned_iff <- tune_iffolk(default_specs(), n_iterations = 80L) tuned_iff$specs$iffolk_transfer   # optimal transfer amount

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: . cheap initial screen committing expensive algorithm. priors good region needed. . Samples n_samples points uniformly hyper-rectangle specify, runs simulation , scores , returns sorted descending score. Cost. 30 simulations ├Ч 5тАУ30 s = 2тАУ15 minutes default settings. Retrieve full specs best row: Strengths. Embarrassingly parallel, needs priors, easy reason , gives partial-dependence plots free -product. Weaknesses. Scales badly dimension (coverage drops fast beyond 5 parameters). DoesnтАЩt exploit structure; two parameters interact, random search wonтАЩt exploit .
code: library(clade)  result <- search_random(   specs_base    = default_specs(),   search_params = list(     mutation_sd   = c(0.01, 0.5),     grass_rate    = c(0.05, 0.8),     n_agents_init = c(20L, 150L)   ),   n_samples  = 30L,   objective  = "genetic_diversity" )  head(result) #>   mutation_sd grass_rate n_agents_init score #> 1       0.312      0.621           127 0.847 #> 2       0.287      0.583           119 0.831 #> 3       0.341      0.548           143 0.819 best_specs <- attr(result, "specs_list")[[1]] cat("Best mutation_sd:", best_specs$mutation_sd, "\n") cat("Best grass_rate:", best_specs$grass_rate, "\n")

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: . want understand achievable set outcomes, just single best point. MAP-Elites fills grid niches behavioural space reports best parameter set niche. . Divides behavioural space (two dimensions get_run_data()$ticks columns) bins. Starts random parameter sets, iteration picks existing archive member, perturbs , runs simulation, places result whichever bin falls тАФ iff bin empty new score beats incumbent. Cost. 300 simulations = 20тАУ90 minutes. Interpreting heatmap. Filled cells show achievable (diversity, population) pairs. colour encodes objective score. Empty cells (grey) combinations simulated parameter set produced. high-diversity, large-population cells empty, combination may intrinsically unattainable. raw archive (result$archive) list length equal number cells. Non-null entries $specs, $score, $behavioural_descriptor. Inspect archive directly: Strengths. Illumination тАФ reveals Pareto-like frontiers impossible regions single-objective methods silently skip . Robust local optima. Weaknesses. Expensive per cell filled. Needs meaningful bin definitions; archive dims donтАЩt carve space usefully, result uninformative. Known issue (0.4.0 backlog). default mutation, MAP-Elites sometimes fills one cell тАФ candidate mutations produce near- identical behavioural descriptors. See s-map-elites audit workaround (wider mutation + broader archive bins).
code: result <- search_map_elites(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   archive_dims = list(     genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),     n_agents          = seq(0, 200, by = 20)   ),   n_iterations = 300L,   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   verbose      = FALSE )  # Archive heatmap: x = genetic_diversity niche, y = n_agents niche, # fill = objective score (NA = empty cell). result$map non_empty <- Filter(Negate(is.null), result$archive) cat("Filled cells:", length(non_empty), "\n") cat("mutation_sd range in archive:",     range(sapply(non_empty, function(x) x$specs$mutation_sd)), "\n")

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: . want single best parameter combination one scalar objective, parameters continuous (discrete categorical choices like brain_type). . Maintains multivariate Gaussian parameter space. generation samples popsize candidates, runs , uses ranked top half update GaussianтАЩs mean covariance. step size (sigma) shrinks search converges. cladeтАЩs search_cmaes() pure-R implementation (Hansen 2006) тАФ external packages required. Cost. n_iterations ├Ч popsize simulations = 200тАУ500, .e.┬а15тАУ90 minutes. Strengths. State art continuous derivative-free optimisation. Converges faster random search, handles ill-conditioned objectives. Treats run_alife() black box тАФ discrete events (death, birth) obstacle. Weaknesses. Returns single point, trade-map. Can get stuck local optima (mitigated running multiple restarts different seeds). DoesnтАЩt handle categorical parameters (brain_type, life_history). Rule thumb choosing CMA-ES MAP-Elites: use CMA-ES care optimum ; use MAP-Elites care landscape looks like around optimum. CMA-ES faster per run single-objective answer; MAP-Elites informative per compute budget multi-objective insight.
code: result <- search_cmaes(   specs_base = default_specs(),   params     = c("mutation_sd", "grass_rate", "crossover_rate"),   objective  = "genetic_diversity",   n_iterations = 20L,   popsize    = 10L )  cat("Best diversity:", result$score, "\n") cat("Best params:\n") print(result$specs[c("mutation_sd", "grass_rate", "crossover_rate")])

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: . CMA-ES region тАЩre sure population survives . Also useful standalone sanity-check setting new scenario. . Runs rectangular 2-parameter grid n_reps replicates per cell. Reports ggplot heatmap coloured viability (green = population survives; red = extinction) tidy data frame. Cost. length(values_x) ├Ч length(values_y) ├Ч n_reps simulations. 5 ├Ч 5 ├Ч 3 = 75 runs, 5тАУ15 minutes. Strengths. Clearest possible picture system alive. Runs fast extinction-prone cells finish early. Cheap enough run every time set new scenario. Weaknesses. 2 parameters time (design тАФ heatmap output). DoesnтАЩt optimise; just shows тАЩs safe optimise.
code: library(clade)  s <- default_specs() s$complex_landscape <- TRUE  vmap <- search_viability(   s,   param_x = "shrub_density",  values_x = seq(0.1, 0.6, by = 0.1),   param_y = "canopy_density", values_y = seq(0.05, 0.35, by = 0.1),   n_reps  = 3L )  vmap$map     # ggplot2 heatmap vmap$data    # data frame with viability, mean_final_pop per cell

text: two scenarios viability mapping particularly worthwhile, clade ships domain-specific tuners combine viability mapping CMA-ES pass inside viable region: tune_complex_landscape() тАФ five landscape parameters + custom objective measuring joint increase wing size, niche diversity, survival. tune_iffolk() тАФ IFfolk transfer rate parliament cost + custom objective rewarding upward trends mean_helper_tendency.

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: . Final-tick refinement objective surface smooth near known good point тАФ want fractional improvement parameter already trust. . Estimates gradient objective respect parameter finite differences, takes step positive direction. Repeats step size drops threshold. Cost. n_iterations ├Ч (n_params ├Ч 2 + 1) simulations per iteration (two finite-difference evaluations per parameter plus one midpoint). Rarely efficient option. Strengths. Simple. Good last 1тАУ5% optimisation CMA-ES found basin. Weaknesses. Gets stuck local optima. Sensitive step size. DoesnтАЩt handle noisy objectives well (cladeтАЩs simulations stochastic, objectives noisy). Usage mirrors CMA-ES signature:
code: result <- search_gradient(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   params       = c("mutation_sd", "grass_rate"),   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   n_iterations = 20L,   step_size    = 0.02 )

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: Julia mature automatic differentiation (Zygote.jl, Enzyme.jl). natural ask whether differentiate run_alife() end--end use gradient descent. . run_alife() contains several operations differentiable: Agent death threshold event: energy < starvation_threshold. Reproduction conditional energy >= min_repro_energy. Action selection uses argmax brain logits. Mating categorical sample. Autodiff either fails trace time silently produces zero gradient. correct class optimisers stochastic discrete-event simulations derivative-free: CMA-ES, MAP-Elites, random sampling. treat simulator black box estimate curvature population statistics.

title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
text: five search functions accept n_cores argument (default 1). Set number R workers want clade fan inner simulations across. n_iterations ├Ч popsize = 200 simulations, 4-core machine can cut wall time ~40 minutes ~10 minutes. Julia caches precompiled simulation package per worker, first call worker pays ~60-second compile cost. batches 10 simulations per worker, overhead amortised; smaller batches, consider running fewer cores using batch_alife() warm Julia sessions first.

what: Choosing the right algorithm тАФ a decision tree
title: Parameter search тАФ algorithms
code: Is the population surviving at all at the starting point? тФЬтФАтФА No  тЖТ search_viability() first. тФФтФАтФА Yes тЖТ Do you want one best answer or a landscape?     тФЬтФАтФА One best answer     тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА Parameters continuous?    тЖТ search_cmaes()     тФВ   тФФтФАтФА Parameters categorical?   тЖТ loop over conditions     тФВ                                   with batch_alife()     тФФтФАтФА A landscape         тФЬтФАтФА 2D trade-offs known in advance  тЖТ search_map_elites()         тФФтФАтФА Exploring without priors        тЖТ search_random()                                               (then iterate)

title: Parameter search тАФ environment-level parameters
text: grass_rate single consequential environmental parameter. Low values (0.02тАУ0.05) give scarce environment exposes life-history trade-offs; high values (0.2тАУ0.5) give abundant environment density-dependent effects dominate. scenario audits dev/audit/fidelity/ sweep explicitly. max_bite new 0.4.0. implements handling time тАФ rich cell stripped one step. matters ecological-release scenarios: max_bite = grass_max, forager finds rich cell wins everything; smaller max_bite, cell sustains multiple grazings across ticks. See kernel-0.4.0.md biology.

Use search_viability() first тАФ see worked example . seasonal_amplitude interacts multiplicatively grass_rate: effective_rate_t = grass_rate ├Ч (1 + seasonal_amplitude ├Ч sin(2╧А t / season_length)). signature s-seasonal lag-season_length/2 anti-correlation grass_coverage.

title: Parameter search тАФ environment-level parameters
text: running CMA-ES parameter region, worth mapping viable region тАФ set parameter combinations population doesnтАЩt go extinct. search_viability() single call returns heatmap tidy data frame. viable region identified, run CMA-ES inside : tune_complex_landscape() pre-configured wrapper: sets five landscape parameters (shrub_density, canopy_density, shrub_energy, canopy_energy, shrub_growth_rate) uses objective_complex_landscape(), measures joint increase wing size, niche diversity, survival.
code: library(clade)  s <- default_specs() s$complex_landscape <- TRUE  vmap <- search_viability(   s,   param_x = "shrub_density",  values_x = seq(0.1, 0.6, by = 0.1),   param_y = "canopy_density", values_y = seq(0.05, 0.35, by = 0.1),   n_reps  = 3L )  vmap$map     # ggplot2 heatmap: green = viable, red = extinct vmap$data    # data frame with viability, mean_final_pop per cell tuned <- tune_complex_landscape(default_specs(), n_iterations = 80L) tuned$specs          # optimal parameter set tuned$history$sigma  # step-size decay тАФ smaller sigma = converged

what: Worked example: CMA-ES on resource parameters
title: Parameter search тАФ environment-level parameters
text: viability isnтАЩt concern just want single best resource environment biological outcome, CMA-ES handful continuous environment parameters cheap: call pattern agent-parameter CMA-ES example; difference parameters pass. search functions donтАЩt care agent/environment distinction тАФ distinction , experimenter, keep conceptual design clean.

text: MAP-Elites especially useful temporal-structure parameters achievable set (mean diversity, amplitude population oscillation) inherently two-dimensional. Reading heatmap: empty cells combinations seasonal regime achieved. high-diversity, high-population cells empty, combination may intrinsically unattainable тАФ seasonality may preclude simultaneous high diversity high density.

title: Parameter search тАФ introduction
text: Every search function accepts either column name (get_run_data()$ticks) custom function takes run environment returns scalar. custom function form important one: separates valid scientific search fitness-hacking exercise. Good objective functions clade typically three properties. 1. measure biology actually claim. scenarioтАЩs biological claim тАЬHamiltonтАЩs rule predicts population rises rB/CтАЭ, objective Spearman correlation rB/C mean_n_agents across grid B C values тАФ population size single parameter point. guards GoodhartтАЩs law, earlier CMA-ES-calibrated regimes scenario vignettes fell victim . 2. penalise degenerate outcomes. run goes extinct tick 50 rewarded transient high diversity. canonical pattern : penalises extinction, smooths score surface, keeps optimiser тАЬhigh-diversity-just--collapseтАЭ trap. 3. insensitive run length . want тАЬequilibrium diversityтАЭ, average second half run rather whole run тАФ first half dominated initial-condition transients. custom_obj can passed unchanged search_random(), search_map_elites(), search_cmaes(), search_gradient().
code: custom_obj <- function(env) {   d   <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   div <- mean(d$genetic_diversity, na.rm = TRUE)   pop <- mean(d$n_agents,          na.rm = TRUE)   if (pop < 10) return(0)      # discard runs that go near-extinct   div * log1p(pop / 50)        # reward diversity weighted by log-population }

title: Parameter search тАФ introduction
text: practical constraint: run_alife() call takes roughly 5тАУ30 seconds modern machine (default grid, 500 ticks). typical search therefore involves ~300 runs costs tens minutes hour. Two ways multiply throughput: Use n_cores > 1 search functions. clade fan inner simulations across R workers. Use smaller max_ticks search (300тАУ500) re-run winning candidate full length (1,000+) confirm signal transient. typically doubles effective throughput. Avoid running CMA-ES MAP-Elites max_ticks > 1000 know winning region; coarse--fine workflow cheaper.
