title: Pace-of-life syndromes
text: models. Pace--life syndromes (R├йale et al.┬а2010) describe co-variation life-history traits, behaviour, physiology along slowтАУfast continuum. Fast-living organisms high metabolic rates, reproduce early often, die young; slow-living organisms invest maintenance, defer reproduction, achieve longer lifespans. Stearns (1992) formalised consequence age-specific mortality schedule: high extrinsic mortality selects early reproduction fast metabolism. scenario fixes metabolic rate across three values isolate direct effect metabolic pace ageтАУreproductionтАУenergy trade-. Key parameters. Expected output. Fast-pace agents (metabolic_rate_init_mean = 2.0) exhibit higher n_births per tick, lower mean_age, higher energy throughput, populations volatile. Slow-pace agents (metabolic_rate_init_mean = 0.5) show lower birth rate, higher mean_age, stable mean_energy. Fast agents dominate productive environments; slow agents persist reliably resources lean. Audit history. pre-0.4.0 audit found metrics flat (mean_age тЙИ 98 regardless rate) hardcoded max_age = 200 cap dominated age schedule. 0.4.0 Tier 2 fix (max_age_scales_with_metabolism = TRUE) makes eff_max_age = max_age / metabolic_rate, cleanly recovers R├йale et al. signal: Spearman(rate, mean_age) = тИТ1.00 across 5 rates. Note: metabolic_rate_evolution must TRUE read init_mean тАФ FALSE, trait hardcoded 1.0. Set mutation_sd = 0 freeze rate init value still reading spec. See dev/audit/fidelity/pace_of_life.md.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  # NOTE: metabolic_rate_evolution must be TRUE to read init_mean # (when FALSE, metabolic_rate is hardcoded to 1.0). Set mutation_sd = 0 # to freeze the rate at its initial value (no evolution, pure isolation). make_run <- function(rate, seed = 99L) {   s <- default_specs()   s$metabolic_rate_evolution      <- TRUE    # needed to read init_mean   s$metabolic_rate_init_mean      <- rate   s$metabolic_rate_mutation_sd    <- 0.0     # freeze: no evolution   s$max_age_scales_with_metabolism <- TRUE   # 0.4.0 Tier 2: max_age тИЭ 1/rate   s$n_agents_init <- 100L   s$grass_rate    <- 0.12   s$max_ticks     <- 500L   s$random_seed   <- seed   get_run_data(run_alife(s))$ticks }  d_slow <- make_run(0.5) d_mid  <- make_run(1.0) d_fast <- make_run(2.0)  df <- rbind(   cbind(d_slow[, c("t", "mean_age", "n_agents")], pace = "Slow (0.5)"),   cbind(d_mid[,  c("t", "mean_age", "n_agents")], pace = "Moderate (1.0)"),   cbind(d_fast[, c("t", "mean_age", "n_agents")], pace = "Fast (2.0)") )  library(patchwork) p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(t, mean_age, colour = pace)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Slow (0.5)" = "#1565C0",     "Moderate (1.0)" = "#2E7D32", "Fast (2.0)" = "#E65100"), name = NULL) +   labs(title = "Slow-pace agents live 4├Ч longer", y = "Mean age") +   theme_minimal() p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(t, n_agents, colour = pace)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Slow (0.5)" = "#1565C0",     "Moderate (1.0)" = "#2E7D32", "Fast (2.0)" = "#E65100"), name = NULL) +   labs(title = "Slow-pace sustains 5├Ч more agents", x = "Tick", y = "n_agents") +   theme_minimal() p1 / p2

title: Population genetics and heritability
text: models. central quantity quantitative genetics narrow-sense heritability, h┬▓ тАФ proportion phenotypic variance trait attributable additive genetic effects (Falconer & Mackay 1996; Lynch & Walsh 1998). trait respond natural selection unless h┬▓ > 0. estimate_heritability() function performs parent-offspring regression pedigree records accumulated simulation: offspring trait values regressed mid-parent values, slope estimates h┬▓. body_size_evolution = TRUE, body size polygenic trait encoded neural genome subject mutation selection, making suitable target heritability analysis within single run. Key parameters. Expected output. h┬▓ body_size substantially positive (0.3тАУ0.7) early run, reflecting strong additive genetic architecture freshly initialised population. directional selection erodes genetic variance, h┬▓ may decline later ticks. 95% confidence interval regression exclude zero. h┬▓ energy expected lower, energy heavily influenced local environment. mean body-size trajectory whose lag-1 autocorrelation proxies narrow-sense heritability. high lag-1 value (>0.9) consistent strong parent-offspring resemblance; direct replacement parent-offspring regression slope. found. Running body_size_evolution = TRUE, 100 agents, 200 ticks (seed 42): body size showed lag-1 autocorrelation 0.985, high heritability proxy consistent strong parentтАУoffspring resemblance. Mean body size evolved 1.001 1.033 (+3.3%) 200 ticks, trait SD = 0.086 across run. high autocorrelation reflects mean_body_size changes smoothly ticks (one generation turns per ticks), parentтАУoffspring regression slope fall range 0.5тАУ0.8 computed directly pedigree records. get_run_data() returns tick-level summary; construct actual parentтАУoffspring regression, collect agentтАЩs body_size parent_id death env$deaths, match offspring parent body size, use lm(offspring_size ~ parent_size).
code: library(clade)  s <- fast_specs()                     # ~66 generations in 2000 ticks s$body_size_evolution <- TRUE  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  # `estimate_heritability()` returns a lag-1 autocorrelation proxy for # narrow-sense heritability on the population mean trajectory. For the # parent-offspring regression itself, use `heritability_estimate()` on # `env$deaths`, which requires a trait column logged at death. h2_proxy <- estimate_heritability(data, trait = "body_size") cat("Heritability proxy (lag-1 autocorrelation) for body_size:",     round(h2_proxy$h2, 3), "\n") cat("n =", h2_proxy$n, "  method =", h2_proxy$method, "\n")  # Mean trajectory тАФ the quantity the proxy is computed on. plot(data$ticks$t, data$ticks$mean_body_size, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean body size",      main = "Body size evolution (proxy for heritable response)")

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork)  make_s <- function(n_pred) {   s <- fast_specs()                   # ~66 generations in 2000 ticks   s$n_predators_init <- n_pred   s }  results <- batch_alife(   list(no_predators = make_s(0L), predators = make_s(10L)),   n_cores = 2L )  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(     t                = d$t,     genetic_diversity = d$genetic_diversity,     mean_energy      = d$mean_energy,     condition        = nm   ) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  p1 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(no_predators = "#4dac26",                                  predators    = "#d01c8b")) +   labs(title = "Predation and neural genome diversity",        x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity", colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal()  p2 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = mean_energy, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(no_predators = "#4dac26",                                  predators    = "#d01c8b")) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Mean energy", colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal()  p1 / p2

title: Predator-prey dynamics
text: models. Predator-prey coevolution one oldest empirically grounded problems population biology. classical Lotka-Volterra equations predict sustained sinusoidal oscillations predator abundance tracks prey abundance quarter-cycle lag (Lotka 1925; Volterra 1926). Crucially, mean-field prediction assumes fixed, mass-action predators. clade, predators carry evolving neural-network genome тАФ learn, mutate, adapt across generations. single change enough break textbook LV signature, specific well-documented way (see тАЬfoundтАЭ ). Initial predators random neural genomes hunt poorly. Prey climb rapidly toward grass-imposed carrying capacity. Predators survive long enough reproduce pass slightly-better--random hunting brains. Arms-race equilibrium (t тЙИ 30+). Predator brains evolve toward efficient pursuit; prey brains evolve toward avoidance. Predators saturate cap (predator_max_agents) stay even prey sparse тАФ efficient hunters extract enough food non-zero prey density maintain cap-limited population. Prey continue oscillate (driven grass renewal ├Ч predation pressure), classic quarter-cycle predator lag textbook LV muted: predator decline phase LV requires suppressed ongoing pursuit-trait evolution. documented behaviour evolutionary predator-prey ABMs (see predator-prey fidelity report cross-reference alifeR prototype, contains explanation ┬з12 showcase vignette). recover textbook sustained sinusoidal LV, need fixed-policy predator (yet exposed spec) non-toroidal patchy spatial structure (Huffaker 1958; future scenario). Calibrated predator-prey regime (seed 42, 1000 ticks). Predators bootstrap 10 cap 100 tick ~30 stay remainder тАФ arms-race equilibrium. Prey climb peak ~460 tick 200, crash ~250 predator pressure builds, continue oscillating amplitude ┬▒30 around mean ~300. classic LV quarter-cycle predator lag muted evolved predators track prey efficiently regardless density. found. calibrated parameters , seed 42, 1000 ticks: predators bootstrap 10 cap 100 tick ~30 remain saturated rest run тАФ arms-race equilibrium, kernel limit hit (multi-seed audit confirmed runs predator_max_agents = 800 show identical saturation behaviour ~6тАУ12 individuals energy granted kills). Prey climb 150 466 tick 200, crash 245 ticks 200тАУ250 cap-limited predators consume faster grass renewal can replace , continue oscillating around ~300 amplitude ┬▒30. Across 10 seeds prey oscillation score (magnitude negative autocorrelation lag-20--lag-100 range tick 100) 0.39 ┬▒ 0.14 тАФ reproducible cycle. Predator oscillation score тЙИ 0 design: evolved, track prey efficiently never enter LV decline phase. expected signature evolutionary predator-prey ABM, matches alifeR R prototypeтАЩs documented behaviour (see alifeR/vignettes/showcase.Rmd ┬з12 тАФ package authorтАЩs explanation evolving predators suppress textbook LV oscillations). Lotka 1925тАЩs mean-field sustained sinusoidal result, longer claim . Full cross-reference table audit protocol: dev/audit/fidelity/predator_prey.md.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  s <- default_specs() s$n_predators_init          <- 10L s$n_agents_init             <- 100L s$grid_rows                 <- 30L s$grid_cols                 <- 30L s$predator_energy_gain      <- 30 s$predator_min_repro_energy <- 50 s$predator_max_agents       <- 100L s$grass_rate                <- 0.20 s$max_ticks                 <- 1000L s$random_seed               <- 42L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  ggplot(data$ticks, aes(x = t)) +   geom_line(aes(y = n_agents,    colour = "Prey"),     linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = n_predators, colour = "Predators"), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Prey" = "#2196F3", "Predators" = "#F44336"),     name   = NULL   ) +   labs(     x     = "Tick",     y     = "Population size",     title = "Evolutionary predator-prey: prey oscillate, predators arms-race to cap"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12)

Agents RL enabled adapt within lifetime local resource distribution. learning_rate_evolution = TRUE, evolved learning rate reflects environmental uncertainty: noisy environments favour lower rates; stable environments favour higher rates. 0.4.1 audit (3 seeds ├Ч 3 BNN sample frequencies ├Ч RL /├Ч 500 ticks). bnn_sample_freq=1 BNN resamples weights every tick, washing REINFORCE gradient updates (null ╬Ф). freq=5 sample persists long enough gradients compound тАФ ╬Фn = +5.2 (тЬЕ). freq=20 sample rigid populations crash. Running RL vs -RL bnn_sample_freq = 1 (old default): mean population 208 vs 207; mean energy 124.8 vs 124.85 тАФ essentially null. title: Scavenging and carrion dynamics

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  base_specs <- function() {   s <- default_specs()   s$n_agents_init <- 100L   s$grid_rows     <- 30L   s$grid_cols     <- 30L   s$grass_rate    <- 0.15   s$max_ticks     <- 400L   s }  s_no <- base_specs(); s_no$scavenging <- FALSE s_sc <- base_specs() s_sc$scavenging        <- TRUE s_sc$carrion_fraction  <- 0.5 s_sc$carrion_decay_rate <- 0.1 s_sc$carrion_eat_gain  <- 3.0  d_no <- get_run_data(run_alife(s_no))$ticks d_sc <- get_run_data(run_alife(s_sc))$ticks  d_no$condition <- "No scavenging" d_sc$condition <- "Scavenging" dat <- rbind(d_no, d_sc)  ggplot(dat, aes(x = t, y = mean_energy, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("No scavenging" = "#E53935", "Scavenging" = "#FB8C00"),     name   = NULL   ) +   labs(     x     = "Tick",     y     = "Mean agent energy",     title = "Scavenging sustains energy budgets under resource scarcity"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12)

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  make_specs <- function(carrion_tp = 0.0) {   s <- default_specs()   s$n_agents_init           <- 200L   s$grid_rows               <- 25L   s$grid_cols               <- 25L   s$grass_rate              <- 0.08   s$max_ticks               <- 300L   s$scavenging              <- TRUE   s$carrion_fraction        <- 0.5   s$carrion_decay_rate      <- 0.1   s$disease                 <- TRUE   s$transmission_prob       <- 0.15   s$disease_seed_prob       <- 0.03   s$carrion_transmission_prob <- carrion_tp   s }  d_safe <- get_run_data(run_alife(make_specs(0.0)))$ticks d_risk <- get_run_data(run_alife(make_specs(0.3)))$ticks  d_safe$condition <- "Carrion safe (prob = 0)" d_risk$condition <- "Carrion risky (prob = 0.3)" dat <- rbind(d_safe, d_risk)  ggplot(dat, aes(x = t, y = n_new_infections, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Carrion safe (prob = 0)" = "#43A047",                "Carrion risky (prob = 0.3)" = "#E53935"),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     x     = "Tick",     y     = "New infections per tick",     title = "Carrion as pathogen reservoir amplifies epidemics"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12)

title: Seasonal dynamics
text: models. Seasonality imposes periodic variation resource availability mortality risk drives boom-bust dynamics across wide range taxa (Boyce 1979). clade, grass productivity oscillates sinusoidally period season_length amplitude seasonal_amplitude. winter phases, grass regrowth slows per-tick death probability (winter_death_prob) adds additional mortality pressure. result structured environment agents must survive periods scarcity reproduce periods abundance тАФ minimal model temperate seasonality. Key parameters. Expected output. Population size (n_agents) track grass coverage short lag, producing recurrent crashes winter recoveries spring. Multiple complete cycles visible 500 ticks given season_length = 100. found. seasonal_amplitude = 0.6, winter_death_prob = 0.01, 100 agents 30├Ч30 grid, 500 ticks (seed 42): population oscillated 13 223 agents (mean 80). Grass coverage tracked 100-tick sinusoidal cycle clearly тАФ minimum ticks 75, 175, 275 (winter phase) maximum 25, 125, 225 (summer). Population lagged grass approximately 15тАУ20 ticks. aggressive parameters (seasonal_amplitude = 0.8, winter_death_prob = 0.05), population crashed 0 ticks mean dropped 35 тАФ parameter calibration matters substantially observing smooth seasonal cycles rather extinction dynamics. Expected output: population size (blue) tracks scaled grass productivity (green) short lag, producing recurrent winter crashes spring recoveries approximately five complete cycles.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)  s <- default_specs() s$seasonal_amplitude <- 0.8 s$season_length      <- 100L s$winter_death_prob  <- 0.05 s$n_agents_init      <- 100L s$grid_rows          <- 30L s$grid_cols          <- 30L s$max_ticks          <- 500L  env  <- run_alife(s) tks  <- get_run_data(env)$ticks  tks$grass_scaled <- tks$grass_coverage /   max(tks$grass_coverage, na.rm = TRUE) *   max(tks$n_agents,       na.rm = TRUE)  dat_long <- pivot_longer(   tks[, c("t", "n_agents", "grass_scaled")],   cols      = c("n_agents", "grass_scaled"),   names_to  = "variable",   values_to = "value" ) dat_long$variable <- ifelse(dat_long$variable == "n_agents",                             "Population size", "Grass coverage (scaled)")  ggplot(dat_long, aes(x = t, y = value, colour = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Population size" = "#1565C0",                "Grass coverage (scaled)" = "#2E7D32"),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     x     = "Tick",     y     = "Value (agents / scaled grass)",     title = "Seasonal boom-bust dynamics"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12)

title: Signals and mate choice

Signal cost + sexual # selection intensity combine to overwhelm the short fast_specs # lifespan. s <- default_specs() s$signal_dims          <- 3L s$signal_cost          <- 0.05 s$mate_choice_mode     <- "preference" s$mate_choice_strength <- 0.7 s$max_ticks            <- 400L s$random_seed          <- 21L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  p1 <- ggplot(data$ticks, aes(t, mean_signal_magnitude)) +   geom_line(colour = "#984ea3") +   labs(title = "Signal elaboration under sexual selection",        x = "Tick", y = "Mean signal magnitude") +   theme_minimal()  p2 <- ggplot(data$ticks, aes(mean_energy, mean_signal_magnitude)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8, colour = "#984ea3") +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "#333333", linewidth = 0.7) +   labs(title = "Signal magnitude vs mean energy (honest signalling check)",        x = "Mean energy", y = "Mean signal magnitude") +   theme_minimal()  print(p1) print(p2)

what: Stress hypermutation тАФ the SOS response
title: Stress hypermutation
text: models. bacteria, severe DNA damage prolonged resource depletion triggers SOS response: suite error-prone polymerases dramatically increase genomic mutation rate (McKenzie & Rosenberg 2001). bet-hedging strategy тАФ mutations deleterious, expanded variance offspring phenotype increases probability least one lineage escape current fitness trough. stress_hypermutation = TRUE, agents whose energy falls stress_threshold apply mutation rate multiplied stress_mutation_multiplier neural genome. Pairing scarce-resource environment (grass_rate = 0.05) ensures stress frequent enough detect effect within tractable run length. Key parameters. Expected output. Compare genetic_diversity trajectories across two conditions. stress hypermutation, diversity spike transiently resource crashes (low mean_energy epochs), followed faster adaptive recovery beneficial mutants sweep. baseline condition show slower, smoother recovery equivalent crashes.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  make_s <- function(hypermut) {   # default_specs() тАФ fast_specs + grass_rate = 0.05 crashes   # completely (n_final тЙИ 0 per crash_audit.R). The stress   # hypermutation regime needs low grass, but fast_specs short   # lifespan can't survive low grass.   s <- default_specs()   s$stress_hypermutation       <- hypermut   s$stress_threshold           <- 20.0   s$stress_mutation_multiplier <- 5.0   s$grass_rate                 <- 0.05   s$max_ticks                  <- 500L   s }  results <- batch_alife(   list(baseline = make_s(FALSE), hypermutation = make_s(TRUE)),   n_cores = 2L )  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(t = d$t, genetic_diversity = d$genetic_diversity, condition = nm) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(baseline = "#878787",                                  hypermutation = "#d6604d")) +   labs(     title    = "Stress hypermutation increases genetic diversity during resource crashes",     subtitle = "grass_rate = 0.05; stress_mutation_multiplier = 5",     x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity", colour = "Condition"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Stress hypermutation
text: baseline result shows stress hypermutation transiently elevates genetic diversity resource crashes accelerates adaptive recovery. go beyond: Kin buffering hypermutation cost Add kin_selection = TRUE. Kin altruism provides energy buffer may reduce often agents fall stress_threshold, dampening hypermutation response. kin selection reduce frequency hypermutation events variance genetic_diversity spikes? Plot spike height vs kin_altruism_r_min across batch_alife(). Tried . stress_hypermutation = TRUE, grass_rate = 0.05, 60 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42: genetic diversity SD = 0.0116 without kin vs 0.0175 kin тАФ kin selection increased diversity spike height. Mean diversity also rose 0.181 0.199. Counter prediction: kin energy transfers dampen hypermutation. Spatially clustered kin groups may concentrate stress events locally, producing sharper (smoother) diversity bursts. Hypermutation ├Ч seasonality Add seasonal_amplitude = 0.7. Resource crashes winter trigger hypermutation; timing diversity spikes align seasonal grass trough? Plot genetic_diversity grass_coverage shared axes test synchrony. Tried . stress_hypermutation = TRUE seasonal_amplitude = 0.7 (50 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42): stress hypermutation condition showed gd = 0.192 vs 0.185 without. mean_mutation_rate field returned NA тАФ Julia backend log hypermutation event timing тАФ direct synchrony testing unavailable current logging. diversity increase (3.8%) consistent seasonal troughs triggering stress hypermutation, tick-level alignment confirmed without additional logging support. Evolved mutation rate Enable mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE. population evolve lower baseline mutation_sd hypermutation available stress-contingent backup? hypermutation analogue Baldwin Effect тАФ contingent machinery substituting constitutive mutation cost. Tried . flags enabled (50 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42): gd = 0.188 vs 0.185 without mutation rate evolution. mean_mutation_rate column returned NA тАФ logged Julia backend тАФ canalization prediction (evolved lower baseline mutation contingent hypermutation available) confirmed. diversity difference consistent additional mutational input, Baldwin-like substitution mechanism untestable current metrics.

Print progress (default FALSE batch mode).

"ann" тАФ standard multilayer perceptron. "ctrnn" тАФ continuous-time recurrent network (Beer 1995). "grn" тАФ sparse gene-regulatory network topology. "transformer" тАФ self-attention (highest capacity, slowest). "synthesis" тАФ symbolic rule extraction evolved weights.

title: Compute pedigree-based relatedness between two agents тАФ compute_relatedness
text: compute_relatedness() estimates coefficient relatedness (r) two agents using parent-ID pedigree chains stored env$agents. algorithm returns r = 0.5 parent-offspring, r = 0.25 full siblings (shared parent), r = 0 otherwise. matches Hamilton's rule coefficients used kin selection module.

what: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Returns named list simulation parameters type-annotated defaults. Every parameter documented . Pass modified copy run_alife() search_map_elites().

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
code: default_specs()

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: named list simulation parameters.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: grid_rows Integer. Number rows grid (default 30). grid_cols Integer. Number columns grid (default 30). toroidal Logical. TRUE (default) wraps grid edges moving one side re-enters opposite side (classic torus). FALSE clamps boundaries, producing true edges тАФ required Huffaker-1958-style spatial-refugia dynamics scenario corner/edge effects matter. Used movement, sensing, dispersal, kin-scan, group-defense, cooperative-breeding code paths via wrap_or_clamp(). n_agents_init Integer. Number agents tick 0 (default 50). max_agents Integer. Hard cap live agents; new offspring rejected exceeded (default 500). max_ticks Integer. Simulation length ticks (default 500).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: energy_init Numeric. Energy agent starts (default 100). energy_max Numeric. Maximum energy agent can hold (default 200). move_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per move action (default 1). idle_cost Numeric. Energy deducted agent move (default 0.5). eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit grass consumed (default 5). max_bite Numeric. 0.4.0. Maximum grass units extracted cell per tick (default 2.0). Implements handling time: rich cell stripped one step, multiple agents can graze cell across ticks. Restores alifeR / MATLAB ancestor's maxbite semantics. Per-tick energy income bounded max_bite * eat_gain. min_repro_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required attempt reproduction (default 120). repro_cost_mode Character. 0.4.0. "proportional" (default) deducts repro_cost_fraction * parent_energy per offspring; "fixed" deducts constant repro_cost. Proportional cost implements Smith & Fretwell (1974) parental investment: parents better condition invest. Fixed mode preserved reproducibility pre-0.4.0 runs. repro_cost Numeric. Energy deducted parent per offspring repro_cost_mode = "fixed" (default 30, ignored mode "proportional"). repro_cost_fraction Numeric (0, 1). 0.4.0. Fraction parent energy paid per offspring repro_cost_mode = "proportional" (default 0.5). offspring_energy_mode Character. 0.4.0. "proportional" (default) sets newborn energy offspring_energy_fraction * cost_paid (Smith & Fretwell quality- quantity); "fixed" sets newborn energy constant offspring_energy (legacy). offspring_energy Numeric. Energy given new offspring offspring_energy_mode = "fixed" (default 60). offspring_energy_fraction Numeric (0, 1). 0.4.0. Fraction cost_paid allocated offspring offspring_energy_mode = "proportional" (default 0.25). starvation_threshold Numeric. Agent dies energy falls value (default 0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: grass_init_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability cell starts grass (default 0.5). grass_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability empty cell grows grass (default 0.05). grass_max Numeric. Maximum grass units per cell (default 5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: brain type controls agents map sensory inputs action decisions. types heritable evolvable. See references entry primary literature. brain_type Character. Currently implemented: "bnn" (default), "ann", "ctrnn", "grn", "random". Listed yet implemented (kernel errors "planned later phases"): "transformer", "synthesis" тАФ reserved names, working architectures 0.5.6. BNN = Bayesian neural network (Neal 1996; Blundell et al. 2015); ANN = multilayer perceptron (Rumelhart et al. 1986); CTRNN = continuous-time recurrent network (Beer 1995); GRN = gene regulatory network (Kauffman 1993); random = null baseline. See vignette("custom-modules") architecture details dev/benchmarks/brain_comparison.R side--side benchmark five working types. hidden_layers Integer vector. Hidden layer widths "ann" "bnn" (default c(8L); gives one hidden layer 8 units). Set c(16L, 8L) two hidden layers. input_radius Integer. Radius Moore neighbourhood used building agent's sensory input vector (default 1L). Radius 1 gives standard 8-cell neighbourhood; radius 2 extends 24-cell neighbourhood. Increasing value expands input vector brain's first-layer width accordingly. n_genes Integer. Number genes "grn" brain type (default 20L). Includes sensory input genes action output genes. transformer_history Integer. Number past sensory inputs transformer attends (default 8L). transformer_heads Integer. Number attention heads (default 2L). synthesis_max_rules Integer. Maximum number -rules per agent (default 10L). ann_weight_values Numeric vector NULL. NULL, every synaptic weight bias snapped nearest value set immediately genome expression (first forward pass generation). Use c(-1, 0, 1) ternary weights c(-1, 1) binary weights. Applies "ann" "bnn" brain types. Biologically motivated evidence biological synapses operate small number discrete strength states (Bhumbra & Bhatt 2020). Also enables symbolic formula distillation evolved ANNs (original MATLAB alife2025usra codebase). Default NULL = continuous weights. ann_regularization Character. Energy penalty brain weight complexity: "none" (default), "weight_magnitude" (L1: deduct lambda * sum(|w|) per tick), "weight_count" (L0-like: deduct lambda * n_active_weights per tick). References: Laughlin et al. (1998) Nature Neuroscience 1:36тАУ41; Attwell & Laughlin (2001) J. Cerebral Blood Flow Metabolism 21:1133тАУ1145. ann_regularization_lambda Numeric. Scale factor regularisation penalty (default 0.001). large value cause weights collapse zero within ticks.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Brain energy cost models metabolic cost neural computation, Polyworld simulation (Yaeger 1994). brain_energy_mode Character. One "none" (cost), "size" (proportional synapse count), "activity" (default; base + activity-scaled cost), "prediction_error" (BNN-; KL divergence prior posterior). Reference: Yaeger (1994) PolyWorld, Artificial Life III, pp 263тАУ298. brain_energy_base Numeric. Fixed cost per synaptic weight per tick (default 0.001). brain_energy_activity Numeric. Scaling factor mean absolute activation brain_energy_mode = "activity" (default 0.5). brain_energy_size_exponent Numeric. Exponent applied brain-size term metabolic cost: size_cost = base * n_weights^exp (default 1.0 = linear, legacy). Set 1.5 Kleiber-style super-linear scaling (Isler & van Schaik 2009 expensive-brain hypothesis) large brains carry disproportionate metabolic weight тАФ sharpens parental-provisioning selection gradient default brain_energy_base without needing scenario-specific override (0.4.3). brain_energy_sigma_scale Numeric. Log-scaled information cost BNN posterior width (sigma). > 0, tick costs scale * mean(max(log(sigma / sigma_min), 0)) energy. Default 0 (cost). Set 0.005тАУ0.1 Baldwin canalisation scenarios. Reference: Aiello & Wheeler (1995) expensive-tissue hypothesis. Added 0.4.1 Tier 5C. bnn_action_noise_scale Numeric [0, 1]. Controls much BNN sigma contributes action noise forward pass: w = mu + scale * sigma * z. 1.0 (default) = full coupling (legacy). 0 = deterministic actions mu; sigma affects learning/cost channel. Added 0.5.5 sigma-action decoupling Baldwin/plasticity scenarios. action_exploration_epsilon Numeric [0, 1]. Epsilon- greedy exploration rate, orthogonal BNN sigma. 0 (default) = pure argmax action logits (legacy). > 0 = tick, probability epsilon agent picks uniformly random action instead greedy one. Intended scenarios set bnn_action_noise_scale = 0: BNN sigma drives learning/cost, exploration comes epsilon-greedy, canalisation (sigma тЖТ 0) can happen without eliminating foraging variability agents need survive. Added 0.5.6. bnn_sigma_lr_scale Numeric [0, 1]. > 0, BNN within-lifetime learning rate bnn_update! mixed constant-per-agent lr lr ├Ч mean(sigma) / bnn_sigma_lr_ref, canalised (low-sigma) agents learn slowly plastic (high-sigma) agents learn fast. Complements bnn_action_noise_scale: decouples sigma action noise, couples sigma learning speed. Makes cost canalisation sit learning rate rather exploration. Default 0 preserves legacy behaviour. Added 0.5.6. bnn_sigma_lr_ref Numeric. Reference sigma bnn_sigma_lr_scale mapping. Defaults 0.5, plasticity_init_mean value unset explicitly. Added 0.5.6.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: clade supports diploid (default) haploid life cycles. diploid path implements full Mendelian genetics independent assortment recombination. ploidy Integer. 1L = haploid; 2L = diploid (default). diploid case every heritable trait (brain weights, body size, immune strength, cooperation level, etc.) two alleles тАУ one maternal, one paternal. expressed phenotype computed dominance_model birth change within lifetime. n_chromosomes Integer. Number chromosome pairs (default 1L). > 1, chromosomes assort independently meiosis (pair segregates probability 0.5). brain genome split evenly across chromosome pairs. crossover_rate Numeric. Expected number crossover events per chromosome pair per meiosis (Poisson distributed; default 1.0). Set 0 disable recombination. dominance_model Character. maternal paternal alleles combine: "additive" (default; mean two alleles), "dominant" (random allele locus), "codominant" (expressed; reported separately get_genome_data()). Reference: Charlesworth & Charlesworth (2010) Elements Evolutionary Genetics, Chapter 5.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian noise added brain weight reproduction (default 0.1). mutation_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, mutation_sd heritable diploid trait evolves (default FALSE). Reference: Sniegowski, Gerrish & Lenski (1997) Evolution high mutation rates experimental populations E. coli, Nature 387:703тАУ705. mutation_sd_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean mutation_sd distribution mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.1). mutation_sd_min Numeric. Minimum allowed mutation_sd (default 0.001). mutation_sd_max Numeric. Maximum allowed mutation_sd (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Within-lifetime learning modifies brain without altering genome. learning_rate_evolution = TRUE, learning rate heritable diploid trait; variation learning_rate across population allows Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896) observed: genomes encode pre-adapted solutions outcompete relying learning. rl_mode Character. Reinforcement learning update rule: "none" (default), "actor_critic" (REINFORCE baseline, Williams 1992; reward = energy delta, applied output weights), "hebbian" (Hebbian potentiation, Hebb 1949; co-active neurons strengthened). Use bnn_sample_freq = 5 BNN brains REINFORCE gradients accumulate across ticks. learning_rate Numeric. Step size within-lifetime weight updates (default 0.01). learning_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, learning_rate heritable diploid trait (default FALSE). Enables study Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896, Hinton & Nowlan 1987). References: Baldwin (1896) American Naturalist 30:441тАУ451; Hinton & Nowlan (1987) Complex Systems 1(3):495тАУ502. learning_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean learning_rate distribution learning_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.01). learning_rate_min Numeric. Minimum allowed learning_rate (default 0.0). learning_rate_max Numeric. Maximum allowed learning_rate (default 0.5). plasticity_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit absolute weight change per tick (default 0.05). Implements metabolic cost synaptic plasticity. Reference: Laughlin, de Ruyter van Steveninck & Anderson (1998) metabolic cost neural information, Nature Neuroscience 1(1):36тАУ41. rl_update_freq Integer. Apply RL update every many ticks (default 1L). lamarckian Logical. TRUE rl_mode "none", within-lifetime RL-updated brain weights written back parent's genome meiosis. Offspring therefore directly inherit learned solution rather rediscovering unmodified starting genome (Darwinian path). distinct epigenetics: epigenetics inherits methylation marks record loci canalized; Lamarckian inheritance copies actual learned weight values heritable material. can active simultaneously (default FALSE). Reference: Baldwin (1896) American Naturalist 30:441тАУ451; Weismann (1892) Das Keimplasma; Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: epigenetics = TRUE, agent carries methylome: Boolean vector length genome. element records whether corresponding locus methylated. Within lifetime, learning events can methylate loci (epigenetic_learning_coupling). reproduction, fraction parent's methylation pattern inherited offspring (epigenetic_inheritance). BNN brains, methylation locus shrinks prior sigma_i (canalization тАУ agent becomes less plastic weight). implements transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) described Jablonka & Lamb (2005). epigenetics Logical. Enable epigenetic methylation TEI (default FALSE). Reference: Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press. epigenetic_learning_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Probability within-lifetime learning event methylates corresponding locus (default 0.10). epigenetic_inheritance Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction parent's methylation marks inherited offspring (default 0.50). remaining marks reset (demethylated) offspring. epigenetic_effect_size Numeric. Factor methylation reduces prior sigma (BNN) mutational variance locus (brain types) (default 0.20). methylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous methylation probability per locus per tick (default 0.001). demethylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous demethylation probability per methylated locus per tick (default 0.002).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: n_predators_init Integer. Number predators tick 0 (default 0L). Predators evolving brains ("ann" default) co-evolve prey. predator_energy_init Numeric. Starting energy predators (default 150). predator_attack_cost Numeric. Energy deducted predator per attack attempt (default 2). predator_kill_gain Numeric. Energy predator gains per successful kill (default 50). predator_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD predator brain weights (default 0.1). max_predators Integer. Hard cap predator population (default 20L). predator_sense_graded Logical. TRUE (default, 0.4.2), prey's predator sensory input distance d 1/(d+1) (closer predators produce stronger signal). FALSE, falls back pre-0.4.2 binary presence signal. effect n_predators_init = 0.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: life_history Character. "iteroparous" (default) "semelparous". Semelparous agents die immediately reproducing. Reference: Stearns (1992) Evolution Life Histories, Oxford . max_age Integer. Maximum lifespan ticks; agents die age regardless energy (default 200L). hard cap applies senescence_rate == 0; Gompertz senescence active, late-life mortality governed stochastic curve instead (0.4.2 behaviour). Set Inf disable. senescence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Gompertz mortality rate; per-tick death probability scales exp(senescence_rate * age) (default 0 = senescence). > 0, supersedes max_age hard cap (0.4.2). Reference: Gompertz (1825) nature function expressive law human mortality, Philosophical Transactions Royal Society 115:513тАУ583. repro_senescence Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick decline reproduction probability age (default 0 = reproductive senescence). life_history_evolution Logical. TRUE, max_age senescence_rate heritable diploid traits (default FALSE). allee_threshold Integer. Minimum local density reproduction (default 0L = Allee effects ). > 0, agents require least many conspecifics Moore neighbourhood reproduce. Reference: Allee (1931) Animal Aggregations, University Chicago Press.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: body_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable body size trait (default FALSE). Body size scales metabolic costs (larger = expensive), foraging capacity (larger = grass per tick), energy storage (larger = higher cap). Reference size 1.0 (effect). Reference: Kleiber, M. (1947) Body size metabolic rate, Physiological Reviews 27(4):511тАУ541. body_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean body size (default 1.0). body_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD body size (default 0.08). body_size_min Numeric. Minimum body size (default 0.3). body_size_max Numeric. Maximum body size (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: brain_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable brain size trait (default FALSE). Implements parental provisioning hypothesis: brain size metabolically costly (expensive brain hypothesis) yet confers cognitive foraging advantage. bootstrapping problem тАФ large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth cognitive advantage can offset тАФ means brain size can evolve parental provisioning (parental_care = TRUE) buffers infancy energy deficit. References: van Schaik et al. (2023) PLoS Biology 21(5):e3002064; Griesser et al. (2023) PNAS 120(31):e2301005120; Song et al. (2025) PNAS 122(8):e2412783122. brain_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean brain size (default 1.0, reference = effect). brain_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD brain size (default 0.05). brain_size_min Numeric. Minimum brain size (default 0.1). brain_size_max Numeric. Maximum brain size (default 3.0). brain_size_cost_scale Numeric. Multiplier metabolic surcharge per unit brain_size - 1.0. Higher values steepen cost curve make bootstrapping problem harder (default 1.0). brain_size_sensing_exponent Numeric. Power applied brain_size scaling grass sensing inputs. brain_size^exponent determines sensing multiplier. Exponent 0 = sensing effect; exponent 1.0 = linear scaling; default 0.3 gives gentle sublinear boost (e.g. brain_size = 2.0 тЖТ 1.23├Ч multiplier). effect brain_size_evolution = FALSE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: metabolic_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable metabolic rate (default FALSE). Scales move_cost idle_cost. metabolic_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean metabolic rate (default 1.0). metabolic_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). metabolic_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.1). metabolic_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 5.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: aging_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable aging rate (default FALSE). Scales Gompertz senescence exponent. aging_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean aging rate (default 1.0). aging_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). aging_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.01). aging_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 10.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: immune_evolution Logical. Enable heritable immune strength trait (default FALSE). immune_strength_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean (default 0.3). immune_strength_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). immune_strength_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.0). immune_strength_max Numeric. Maximum (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: disease Logical. Enable SIR disease dynamics (default FALSE). Reference: Kermack & McKendrick (1927) Contributions mathematical theory epidemics, Proceedings Royal Society 115(772):700тАУ721. disease_seed_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability agent initially infected tick 1 (default 0.01). transmission_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability transmission susceptible neighbour per infected-susceptible adjacent pair per tick (default 0.1). Effective transmission = transmission_prob * (1 - receiver's immune_strength). disease_duration Integer. Ticks agent remains infectious (default 10L). immune_duration Integer. Ticks immunity recovery (default 20L). , agent returns Susceptible. disease_energy_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per tick infected (default 5). disease_death_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability death infected (default 0.02). Scaled (1 - immune_strength) immune_evolution = TRUE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: kin_selection Logical. Enable kin altruism (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton (1964) genetical evolution social behaviour & II, Journal Theoretical Biology 7:1тАУ52. kin_altruism_cost Numeric. Energy transferred donor (default 2.0). Hamilton's rule requires benefit * r > cost. kin_altruism_benefit Numeric. Energy received recipient (default 10.0). kin_altruism_r_min Numeric. Minimum pedigree relatedness r required trigger altruism (default 0.25 = half-siblings). kin_altruism_min_donor_energy Numeric. Donor must least energy donate (default 50).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: cooperation_evolution Logical. Enable heritable cooperation level (default FALSE). Uses spatial public goods game (Nowak & May 1992). Reference: Nowak & May (1992) Evolutionary games spatial chaos, Nature 359:826тАУ829. cooperation_multiplier Numeric. Payoff multiplier M public goods game (default 2.0). cooperator pays cost C; group receives M * sum(contributions) / n_local. cooperation selectively favoured, M must exceed group size. cooperation_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean cooperation level (default 0.5). cooperation_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). cooperation_cost Numeric. Per-tick energy cost paid cooperator (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: dispersal_evolution Logical. Enable heritable natal dispersal (default FALSE). agent carries dispersal_tendency trait (per-tick probability moving away birthplace). Reduces inbreeding kin competition. Reference: Ronce, O. (2007) feel like rolling stone? Annual Review Ecology, Evolution, Systematics 38:231тАУ253. dispersal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per dispersal step (default 2.0). Agents energy <= 2 x dispersal_cost disperse. dispersal_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean dispersal tendency (default 0.1). dispersal_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD dispersal tendency (default 0.02). dispersal_min Numeric. Minimum dispersal tendency (default 0.0). dispersal_max Numeric. Maximum dispersal tendency (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: parental_care Logical. Enable altricial parental care (default FALSE). Offspring remain parent graduation. care_duration Integer. Ticks offspring remain parental care (default 5L). care_cost_per_offspring Numeric. Energy cost per carried offspring per tick (default 2.0). max_clutch_size Integer. Maximum offspring per reproductive event (default 1L). neonatal_foraging_deficit Numeric [0, 1]. Reduction foraging efficiency (effective max_bite) newborns first neonatal_deficit_duration ticks life (default 0.0). Creates selection gradient parental provisioning тАФ unprovisioned newborns forage effectively, provisioned ones fed parent via feeding_rate. Reference: Aiello & Wheeler (1995); Isler & van Schaik (2009) expensive brain hypothesis. neonatal_deficit_duration Integer. many ticks neonatal foraging deficit applies (default 10L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: signal_dims Integer. Number signal dimensions (default 0L = ). > 0, agent evolves heritable signal vector preference vector; mates chosen proximity signal preference. signal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit signal magnitude per tick (default 0.1). Models honest signalling costs (Zahavi 1975). Reference: Zahavi (1975) Mate selection тАУ selection handicap, Journal Theoretical Biology 53(1):205тАУ214. signal_toxicity_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Strength aposematic pleiotropy first signal dimension heritable toxicity trait. 0 (default) signal toxicity evolve independently; 1, signal[1] locked agent's toxicity value predators can learn reliable honest aposematic signal. Required close Bates (1862) / M├╝ller (1879) feedback loop clade. Active mimicry = TRUE signal_dims > 0. Added 0.4.4.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: coevolving_parasites Logical. Enable genotype-matched virulence module (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton, W.D. (1980) Sex versus non-sex versus parasite. Oikos 35:282тАУ290. parasite_match_mode Character. One "auto" (default), "continuous", "discrete". Continuous mode (0.5.0) uses Euclidean distance signal vector tracks host population centroid lag тАФ mean-tracking variant reproduce canonical Red Queen. Discrete mode (0.5.1) uses Hamming distance binary-allele parasite_haplotype trait Mendelian inheritance тАФ canonical Hamilton mechanism. "auto" picks discrete n_parasite_loci > 0, else continuous. parasite_virulence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Rate collective parasite genotype tracks host majority tick (default 0.1 тЙИ 10-tick lag). parasite_pressure Numeric. Maximum per-tick energy drain parasite infection, applied hosts exactly matching parasite haplotype (default 0.5). parasite_distance_scale Numeric. Continuous-mode Gaussian falloff scale (default 1.0; ignored discrete mode). n_parasite_loci Integer. Number binary loci heritable parasite_haplotype trait (default 0 = continuous mode ). > 0, agent carries Vector{Int32} length, inherited Mendelian-style diploid, clonal + per-locus mutation haploid. 0.5.1. parasite_mutation_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-locus mutation rate (allele flip) inheritance (default 0.01). parasite_discrete_exponent Numeric. Exponent controlling Hamming-distance falloff: penalty = pressure * ((n_loci - hamming) / n_loci)^exp (default 4.0). Higher exponents concentrate pressure near-matching hosts let mismatched hosts escape cleanly.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: mimicry Logical. Enable heritable toxicity trait predator signal learning (default FALSE). Implements Mullerian Batesian mimicry via Rescorla-Wagner learning predators. Reference: Rescorla & Wagner (1972) theory Pavlovian conditioning, Classical Conditioning II, Appleton-Century-Crofts, pp 64тАУ99.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: niche_construction Logical. Enable shelter building (default FALSE). Shelters reduce predation slow grass regrowth. Reference: Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) Niche Construction: Neglected Process Evolution, Princeton . shelter_build_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability building shelter unit per tick eligible (default 0.1). shelter_max_depth Integer. Maximum shelter depth per cell (default 5L). shelter_min_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required build (default 80). shelter_decay_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability shelter unit decays (default 0.05).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: scavenging Logical. Enable carrion dynamics (default FALSE). Dead agents deposit body mass carrion; scavengers consume . carrion_fraction Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction energy_init deposited carrion upon agent death (default 0.5). Uses energy_init agent$energy death, body mass change starvation. carrion_decay_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick fraction carrion decomposes (default 0.1). carrion_eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit carrion consumed (default 3).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: group_defense Logical. Enable dilution-effect predator avoidance (default FALSE). Per-agent predation probability decreases local group size. Reference: Foster & Treherne (1981) Evidence dilution effect selfish herd fish predation marine insect, Nature 293:466тАУ467.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: habitat_preference_evolution Logical. Enable heritable habitat preference (default FALSE). Preference encodes optimal local grass density; agents attracted cells matching preference. Reference: Fretwell & Lucas (1970) territorial behavior factors influencing habitat distribution birds, Acta Biotheoretica 19(1):16тАУ36.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: seasonal_amplitude Numeric. Amplitude sinusoidal grass_rate modulation (default 0 = ). grass_rate_t = grass_rate * (1 + seasonal_amplitude * sin(2 * pi * t / seasonal_period)). seasonal_period Integer. Period seasonal cycle ticks (default 100L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: speciation Logical. Enable reproductive isolation based genome distance (default FALSE). Agents mate conspecifics (genome distance < isolation_threshold). Reference: Gavrilets (2004) Fitness Landscapes Origin Species, Princeton . isolation_threshold Numeric. Maximum genome distance successful mating (default 0.45). Species inferred hierarchical clustering genome distances logging tick.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: social_learning Logical. Enable copying output-layer weights successful neighbours (default FALSE). Implements prestige bias. Reference: Laland (2004) Social learning strategies, Learning Behavior 32(1):4тАУ14. social_learning_freq Integer. Apply social learning every many ticks (default 10L). social_learning_rate Numeric. Fraction neighbour weights copied (default 0.1).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: world_evolution = TRUE, environment parameters listed world_params_to_evolve treated evolvable quantities change via random drift tick, allowing environment co-evolve agents. extends MAP-Elites search joint agent-environment space. world_evolution Logical. Enable world parameter evolution (default FALSE). world_mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations world parameters per tick (default 0.02). world_params_to_evolve Character vector. Names parameters default_specs() treat evolvable. Example: c("grass_rate", "disease_death_prob"). Defaults character(0) (none).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Adds shrub canopy resource layers top ground grass. Canopy access requires wing_size >= canopy_threshold (heritable). Designed break standard brain-size x longevity correlation. Reference: Liedtke & Fromhage (2019); Isbell (2006). complex_landscape Logical. Enable multi-layer resources (default FALSE). shrub_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial fraction cells shrub resource (default 0.3). shrub_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth shrubs (default 0.03). shrub_energy Numeric. Maximum energy shrub cell provides (default 20.0). canopy_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial canopy coverage (default 0.15; trees sparse). canopy_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth canopy (default 0.005; trees grow slowly). canopy_energy Numeric. Maximum energy canopy cell provides (default 50.0; high energy density aerial specialists). canopy_threshold Numeric. Minimum wing_size needed canopy access (default 0.6). wing_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean wing_size (default 0.0; ground-bound founders). wing_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD wing_size (default 0.05). wing_size_min Numeric. Minimum wing size (default 0.0). wing_size_max Numeric. Maximum wing size (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Implements assortative mating invasion range front, causing dispersal-enhancing alleles accumulate frontier without requiring fitness advantage sedentary conspecifics. Reference: Shine et al. (2011) evolutionary process assembles phenotypes space rather time. PNAS 108:5708тАУ5711. Requires dispersal_evolution = TRUE effect. spatial_sorting Logical. Enable range-front mate preference high-dispersal partners (default FALSE). sorting_front_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction maximum range radius defines "front" (default 0.75; outermost 25%). sorting_mating_boost Numeric. Multiplier applied dispersal_tendency scoring mates front (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: IFfolk = offspring + sum(r x relative's offspring). Agents transfer energy energy-depleted kin. Optional parliament suppression penalises defectors cooperative populations. Reference: Fromhage & Jennions (2019) strategic reference gene. Proc R Soc B 286:20190459. doi:10.1098/rspb.2019.0459 iffolk_selection Logical. Enable IFfolk energy transfers (default FALSE). iffolk_r_min Numeric. Minimum relatedness coefficient relative count (default 0.125 = cousin). iffolk_radius Integer. Spatial search radius relatives grid cells (default 5L). iffolk_transfer Numeric. Maximum energy transferred per altruistic act (default 3.0). iffolk_min_energy Numeric. Minimum donor energy required transfer (default 60.0). parliament_suppression Logical. Enable parliament--genes penalty defectors (negative helper_tendency) surrounded cooperators (default FALSE). parliament_cost Numeric. Energy cost per tick defectors cooperative neighbourhoods (default 0.5).

what: Fixed patch (stable fitness peak тАФ Baldwin Effect demonstration)
title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: Places one grid cells whose grass value reset fixed_patch_value every grow_grass! call, creating permanent high-value resource never depletes. gives natural selection stable fitness peak: agents whose genome encodes fixed policy navigating patch always outperform agents must re-discover exploration lifetime. result genetic assimilation patch-navigation strategy тАФ computational Baldwin Effect described Hinton \& Nowlan (1987). Without module, foraging fitness landscape shifts continuously (stochastic grass, population density feedbacks) exploration remains evolutionarily stable strategy (mean_prior_sigma rises ceiling). Reference: Hinton, G.E. \& Nowlan, S.J. (1987) learning can guide evolution. Complex Systems 1(3):495тАУ502. fixed_patch Logical. Enable stable resource patch (default FALSE). fixed_patch_value Numeric. Grass value maintained patch cells tick (default 5.0, equal grass_max). Values grass_max permitted тАФ cap grow_grass! apply patch uses direct assignment. fixed_patch_x Integer NA_integer_. Column index patch centre (1-indexed). NA_integer_ resolves grid centre column (default). fixed_patch_y Integer NA_integer_. Row index patch centre (1-indexed). NA_integer_ resolves grid centre row (default). fixed_patch_radius Integer. Chebyshev radius patch: 0L = single cell; 1L = 3├Ч3 block; 2L = 5├Ч5 block (default 0L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade тАФ default_specs
text: log_freq Integer. Log population-level statistics every many ticks (default 1L; set higher reduce memory use long runs). log_genomes Logical. Log flattened genome vectors get_genome_data()$genomes log tick (default FALSE; memory intensive large populations). random_seed Integer NA_integer_. Seed Julia's RNG. NA_integer_ uses random seed (default).

what: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
text: estimate_heritability() returns coarse estimate narrow-sense heritability (\(h^2\)) quantitative trait logged per tick run_alife(). estimator lag-1 temporal autocorrelation population mean, $$\hat{h}^2 \approx \mathrm{cor}(\bar{z}_t,\, \bar{z}_{t+1}),$$ used proxy parent-offspring regression (Falconer & Mackay 1996, ch. 10). proxy reasonable () trait directional stabilising selection, (ii) generation overlap moderate, (iii) logging interval short relative generation time. exact quantitative-genetic estimate reported one. exact estimate requires parent-offspring pairs, clade currently log.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
code: estimate_heritability(run_data, trait = "body_size")

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
text: run_data list returned get_run_data() (must contain $ticks column paste0("mean_", trait)). trait Character. trait name (without mean_ prefix). Defaults "body_size". column form mean_<trait> run_data$ticks supported (e.g. "immune_strength", "metabolic_rate", "learning_rate").

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
text: list components: $h2 Numeric. Lag-1 autocorrelation mean_<trait>. Returns NA_real_ series fewer three usable values zero variance. $method Character constant "lag1_autocorrelation". $trait trait name supplied caller. $n Integer. Number paired observations used. $note Character. reminder proxy exact estimate requires parent-offspring logging.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
text: Falconer, D.S. & Mackay, T.F.C. (1996) Introduction Quantitative Genetics, 4th ed. Longman, Harlow.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series тАФ estimate_heritability
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ env  <- run_alife(default_specs()) rd   <- get_run_data(env) estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "body_size") estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "immune_strength") } # }

what: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios тАФ fast_specs
title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios тАФ fast_specs
text: Returns default_specs() calibrated fast generational turnover тАФ 66 generations 2000 ticks (vs ~2.6 defaults). Use preset whenever scenario tests prediction trait evolution across generations (plasticity, Baldwin effect, mimicry, mating systems, dispersal evolution, etc.).

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios тАФ fast_specs
code: fast_specs()

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios тАФ fast_specs
text: specs list calibrated fast evolutionary dynamics.

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios тАФ fast_specs
text: timescale calibration based MATLAB ancestor's parameters (Bulitko 2023), ran ~40x faster generations clade's defaults agents started reproduction energy short minReproductionAge. See dev/docs/timescale-analysis.md full analysis. Key changes default_specs(): max_age 30L (vs 200L). Short lifespan forces generational turnover biologically realistic rate small organism (e.g., Drosophila, small rodent). min_repro_energy 60 (vs 120). Lower threshold means agents breed ~10 ticks foraging, ~100. min_repro_age 3L (vs 0L). Minimum maturation age prevents newborn-immediately-reproducing artefacts. grass_rate 0.20 (vs 0.05). Adequate food sustain viable population ~65 agents fast turnover. max_ticks 2000L. gen time ~30 ticks, gives ~66 generations тАФ adequate evolutionary predictions. n_agents_init 80L. Moderate starting population. max_agents 400L. Room population growth. grid_rows, grid_cols 30L. Standard grid.

title: Full preset specs for publication-quality runs тАФ full_specs

what: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
text: Creates 0/1 wall matrix use run_alife(). Five landscape types available, ranging completely open structured corridor networks. Pass result prepare_map() validate dimensions use.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
code: generate_map(   type = "random_cluster",   grid_rows = 50L,   grid_cols = 50L,   p_wall = 0.3,   scale = 4,   corridor_width = 2L,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
text: type Character; one "open", "patchy", "random_cluster", "gaussian_field", "corridors". Default "random_cluster". grid_rows Integer; number grid rows. Default 50. grid_cols Integer; number grid columns. Default 50. p_wall Numeric (0, 1); target proportion wall cells. Default 0.3. Ignored "open". scale Positive numeric; spatial scale wall clustering (sigma Gaussian, autocorrelation range nlm_gaussianfield). Default 4. corridor_width Integer; open-corridor width cells ("corridors" ). Default 2. seed Integer NULL; random seed. Default NULL.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
text: integer matrix (0 = open, 1 = wall) dimensions grid_rows x grid_cols. Assign specs$wall_map calling run_alife(), pass prepare_map() first.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
text: Types "random_cluster" "gaussian_field" use NLMR package installed; otherwise pure-R Gaussian-smoothed fallback used. NLMR removed CRAN; install ropensci/NLMR (e.g. remotes::install_github("ropensci/NLMR")) want higher-quality neutral-landscape generators.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map тАФ generate_map
code: map <- generate_map("random_cluster", grid_rows = 20L, grid_cols = 20L,                     p_wall = 0.25, seed = 1L) mean(map == 0L)  # fraction open #> [1] 0.75

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs тАФ grid_specs
text: base specs list (default_specs(), fast_specs(), etc.) use template combination. ... Named vectors / lists candidate values parameter. example, grass_rate = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), mutation_sd = c(0.02, 0.05) generates 3 ├Ч 2 = 6-cell grid. String-typed parameters can also passed (e.g. life_history = c("iteroparous", "semelparous")). seed_from Integer NULL. provided, overrides cell's random_seed seed_from + 0L, seed_from + 1L, ... grid run reproducible. Default 1L.

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs тАФ grid_specs
text: named list specs. Names encode parameter values, e.g. "grass_rate=0.1;mutation_sd=0.02".

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs тАФ grid_specs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ base <- fast_specs() specs_list <- grid_specs(base,                          grass_rate   = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3),                          mutation_sd  = c(0.02, 0.05)) results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 6L) } # }

what: Plot genetic diversity over the run тАФ plot_diversity
title: Plot genetic diversity over the run тАФ plot_diversity
text: Draws trajectory mean pairwise genome distance (genetic diversity) , body-size evolution active, coefficient variation body size proxy phenotypic diversity. series scaled 0-1 range visual comparison. common pattern high diversity founders, selective sweep reduces diversity best foraging strategy spreads, partial recovery population niches. Permanent low diversity indicates genetic drift clonal selection; permanently high diversity indicates balancing selection frequency-dependent dynamics.

title: Plot genetic diversity over the run тАФ plot_diversity
code: plot_diversity(run_data)

title: Plot genetic diversity over the run тАФ plot_diversity
text: run_data list returned get_run_data(). Must contain $ticks data frame minimum t, n_agents, genetic_diversity.

text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

text: Plot genetic diversity run

what: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run тАФ plot_module_metrics
title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run тАФ plot_module_metrics
text: Detects optional simulation modules active run inspecting logged tick columns run_data$ticks, assembles six panels patchwork grid. panels modules produced non-zero data included: Predators n_predators тАУ line plot predator population time. Species n_species тАУ step plot species count; shown speciation active (max > 1). Traits Overlay mean_toxicity, mean_plasticity, mean_helper_tendency three coloured lines. Signals mean_signal_magnitude тАУ line plot; shown signal evolution active (max > 0). Parental care n_juveniles тАУ line plot juveniles care; shown max > 0. Mimicry n_toxic_attacks n_avoided_attacks тАУ two lines showing attacks versus avoidance events; shown mimicry attacks occurred (max n_toxic_attacks > 0). fewer two panels active, single informative placeholder plot returned instead.

title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run тАФ plot_module_metrics
code: plot_module_metrics(run_data)

title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run тАФ plot_module_metrics
text: run_data list returned get_run_data(). Must contain $ticks data frame. new module columns (n_predators, n_helpers, mean_signal_magnitude, mean_toxicity, mean_plasticity, mean_helper_tendency, n_species, n_juveniles, n_toxic_attacks, n_avoided_attacks) used present; absent columns silently skipped.

title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run тАФ plot_module_metrics
text: patchwork::wrap_plots() object six panels arranged three columns, single ggplot2::ggplot() placeholder fewer two panels active.

title: Plot genome PCA to reveal population genetic structure тАФ plot_tsne_genomes
text: Takes genome data logged get_run_data() log_genomes = TRUE set run, draws random sample n_agents rows, runs principal components analysis genome weight matrix, plots PC1 versus PC2 coloured tick show genetic structure shifts time. genome data absent contain fewer four rows, placeholder ggplot explanatory message returned instead.

title: Plot genome PCA to reveal population genetic structure тАФ plot_tsne_genomes
code: plot_tsne_genomes(run_data, n_agents = 50L, perplexity = 15, ...)

title: Plot genome PCA to reveal population genetic structure тАФ plot_tsne_genomes
text: run_data list returned get_run_data(). log_genomes = TRUE active, list contains $genomes data frame id column, t column, one numeric column per genome weight. absent function returns placeholder. n_agents Integer. Maximum number genome rows sample running PCA. Keeps computation tractable large logs. Default: 50L. perplexity Numeric. Unused; retained API forward-compatibility future t-SNE upgrade. Default: 15. ... Currently unused. Reserved forward compatibility.

what: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap тАФ plot_weight_heatmap
title: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap тАФ plot_weight_heatmap
text: Renders weight matrix ANN-format brain (list layers element, layer $W $b) diverging blue-white-red tile heatmap. Blue = strong inhibitory connections, red = strong excitatory connections, white = near-zero weights. One panel per layer. Agents strong selection often show structured weight matrices (certain input--hidden connections consistently strong) compared near-random patterns newly initialised founders.

title: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap тАФ plot_weight_heatmap
code: plot_weight_heatmap(ann, title = "Neural genome")

title: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap тАФ plot_weight_heatmap
text: ann brain list $layers element, returned env$agents[[]]$brain ANN brain types. Must R list (JuliaConnectoR proxy). title Character scalar prepended panel title. Default: "Neural genome".

title: Validate and resize a habitat map тАФ prepare_map
code: prepare_map(map, specs)

title: Validate and resize a habitat map тАФ prepare_map
text: map Integer numeric matrix; raw wall matrix. specs Named list; simulation parameters default_specs(). Used dimension checking ($grid_rows, $grid_cols).

title: Validate and resize a habitat map тАФ prepare_map
text: integer matrix dimensions specs$grid_rows x specs$grid_cols values 0 1.

title: Validate and resize a habitat map тАФ prepare_map
code: specs   <- default_specs() specs$grid_rows <- 20L; specs$grid_cols <- 20L raw_map <- matrix(0L, nrow = 20L, ncol = 20L) raw_map[1L, ] <- 1L; raw_map[20L, ] <- 1L map <- prepare_map(raw_map, specs) sum(map)   # number of wall cells #> [1] 40

what: Quick preset specs for fast exploratory runs тАФ quick_specs
title: Quick preset specs for fast exploratory runs тАФ quick_specs
text: Returns default_specs() smaller grid, fewer agents, shorter run length. Use rapid prototyping parameter sweeps exact biological accuracy less important turnaround time.

title: Quick preset specs for fast exploratory runs тАФ quick_specs
code: quick_specs()

title: Quick preset specs for fast exploratory runs тАФ quick_specs
text: specs list identical default_specs() except: n_agents_init 50L max_ticks 200L grid_rows, grid_cols 20L ├Ч 20L

title: Quick preset specs for fast exploratory runs тАФ quick_specs
text: Typical wall time: ~30 seconds per run (Julia warm-excluded).

what: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
text: Injects R function clade simulation tick loop specified hook point. function receives simplified environment snapshot (list(agents, grass, t, specs)) must return (modified unmodified). Multiple modules may registered hook point; run registration order.

title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
code: register_module(fn, when = "post_tick", name = NULL)

title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
text: fn function signature function(env) -> env. Character; one "pre_tick", "post_agents", "post_tick", "post_reproduce". Default "post_tick". name Character label module (optional; used list_modules() output).

title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
text: Invisibly NULL. Side-effects: adds module registry.

title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module

title: Register a custom module function тАФ register_module
code: # Kill any agent older than 100 ticks register_module(   fn   = function(env) {     for (i in seq_along(env$agents)) {       if (isTRUE(env$agents[[i]]$age > 100)) env$agents[[i]]$alive <- FALSE     }     env   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "max_age_100" ) clear_modules()

what: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
text: run_alife() primary entry point clade. sends specs Julia , runs specs$max_ticks ticks entirely Julia, returns environment list containing final agent population logged statistics. Julia session started automatically first call.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
code: run_alife(specs = default_specs(), verbose = TRUE)

title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
text: specs named list simulation parameters, typically default_specs() modifications. parameters documented default_specs(). verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE). Pass FALSE batch runs testing.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
text: env list components: $agents list agent lists, one per surviving agent. $t Final tick number (equals specs$max_ticks). $specs specs list used run (may differ input world_evolution = TRUE). $progress data frame per-tick logged statistics (get_run_data(env)$ticks). $deaths data frame per-death records (get_run_data(env)$deaths). $genome_log list per-tick genome matrices (non-NULL specs$log_genomes = TRUE).

title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
text: R-Julia boundary crossed per call regardless simulation length agent count. contrasts Rcpp-based simulators (including alifeR) data marshalled across R-C++ boundary every tick.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation тАФ run_alife
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs <- default_specs() env   <- run_alife(specs) data  <- get_run_data(env) plot_run(data)  # Diploid run specs$ploidy <- 2L env2 <- run_alife(specs)  # BNN with epigenetics specs$brain_type  <- "bnn" specs$epigenetics <- TRUE env3 <- run_alife(specs) } # }

what: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
text: Implements agent-based model Smaldino & McElreath (2016). "Labs" (agent groups) heritable research_power (W; probability detecting real effect hypothesis test true) research_effort (e; methodological rigour, lowers per-test false-positive rate). tick, lab tests n_studies_per_tick hypotheses, priori true probability base_rate_true (b). Pub counts

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
code: run_bad_science(   n_labs = 200L,   n_ticks = 500L,   n_studies_per_tick = 5L,   replication_rate = 0,   replication_penalty = 5,   alpha_base = 0.5,   base_rate_true = 0.3,   research_power_init_mean = 0.3,   research_effort_init_mean = 0.8,   mutation_sd = 0.05,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
text: n_labs Integer; number labs. Default 200. n_ticks Integer; number evolutionary ticks run. Default 500. n_studies_per_tick Integer; studies produced per lab per tick. Default 5. replication_rate Numeric [0, 1]; probability per tick lab attempts replicate one random neighbour's published findings. failed replication (original false positive) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. Default 0. replication_penalty Numeric \(\geq 0\); publication credits subtracted original lab replication attempt fails. Set 0 recover "replication without cost" behaviour. Default 5. alpha_base Numeric (0, 1]; per-test false-positive rate zero effort. FPR effort e alpha_base * (1 - e). Default 0.5, matching Smaldino & McElreath's \(\alpha_0\). base_rate_true Numeric (0, 1); prior probability tested hypothesis genuinely true. Default 0.3. Higher values mean larger share publications true positives, sharpens replication's ability discriminate low-effort labs; low values (< 0.15) swamp replication's signal almost every publication false positive regardless effort. research_power_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research power W. Default 0.3. research_effort_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research effort e. Default 0.8. mutation_sd Numeric; standard deviation Gaussian mutation applied traits reproduction. Default 0.05. seed Integer NULL; random seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
text: data frame one row per tick columns: t Tick number. mean_power Mean research_power across labs. mean_effort Mean research_effort across labs. mean_fpr Mean false-positive rate alpha across labs. total_publications Total publications produced tick. failed_replications Number failed replication attempts.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
text: true positives  ~ Binomial(n_studies * b,       W) false positives ~ Binomial(n_studies * (1 - b), alpha(e)) alpha(e) = alpha_base * (1 - e). per-test FPR depends effort , W, matching Smaldino & McElreath's formulation. Labs publications reproduce higher rates. replication_rate > 0, lab probability per tick replicating random peer's random finding; failed replication (original false positive, happens probability alpha(1-b) / (Wb + alpha(1-b))) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. large enough penalty, replication culture selects low-effort labs slows reverses evolution high FPR.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
text: Smaldino, P.E. & McElreath, R. (2016) natural selection bad science. Royal Society Open Science 3:160384. doi:10.1098/rsos.160384

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice тАФ run_bad_science
code: result <- run_bad_science(n_ticks = 200L, seed = 1L) plot(result$t, result$mean_fpr, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean false-positive rate",      main = "Evolution of bad science")
