what: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
text: Optimises scalar objective function simulation parameter space using pure-R implementation Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES; Hansen & Ostermeier 2001). Unlike MAP-Elites, CMA-ES finds single optimal parameter set adapting search distribution local curvature objective landscape.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
code: search_cmaes(   specs_base,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 200L,   popsize = NULL,   sigma0 = 0.3,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). objective Character function (search_map_elites()). params Character vector positive numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). n_iterations Integer. Maximum CMA-ES generations (default 200L). popsize Integer NULL. CMA-ES population size lambda. NULL (default), uses standard formula max(8, 4 + floor(3 * log(n_params))). sigma0 Numeric. Initial step size log scale (default 0.3). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores candidate evaluation (default 1L). Uses parallel::makeCluster() PSOCK workers (one R session + Julia per worker) > 1. parallel::mclapply 0.5.6 path silently deadlocked JuliaConnectoR fork-safe тАФ see dev/docs/parallelism-audit.md. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
text: list : $specs Best specs encountered across generations. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per generation columns generation, evals, best_score, mean_score, sigma.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
text: Parameters optimised log scale positive constraints always satisfied. generation evaluates lambda candidate parameter sets, selects best mu = lambda/2, updates mean, step size, covariance matrix. external packages required.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
text: Hansen, N. & Ostermeier, . (2001) Completely derandomized self-adaptation evolution strategies. Evolutionary Computation 9(2):159-195. Hansen, N. (2006) CMA evolution strategy: comparing review. : Towards New Evolutionary Computation, Springer, pp 75-102.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters тАФ search_cmaes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_cmaes(   default_specs(),   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   params       = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 50L ) result$specs$grass_rate result$history   # one row per generation } # }

what: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
text: Optimises scalar objective respect named numeric simulation parameters using forward-difference gradient ascent. gradient coordinate estimated re-running run_alife() one parameter perturbed epsilon log scale, comparing resulting objective baseline run. Updates applied log scale positive parameters remain positive.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
code: search_gradient(   specs_base,   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps = 20L,   epsilon = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). params Character vector numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise. function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. n_steps Integer. Number gradient ascent steps (default 20L). epsilon Numeric. Finite-difference step log scale (default 0.05). learning_rate Numeric. Log-scale step size parameter updates (default 0.1). n_cores Integer. Reserved future parallel finite-difference evaluation; currently unused (default 1L). verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE).

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
text: list components: $specs Best specs encountered across gradient steps. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per step columns step, score, one column per optimised parameter.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
text: implementation intentionally backend-agnostic: treats run_alife() black box requires (n_steps + 1) * n_params simulation calls per run. true gradient--simulation approach using Zygote.jl automatic differentiation Julia backend, see deferred Phase 4b plan.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
text: Spall, J.C. (1998) overview simultaneous perturbation method efficient optimization. Johns Hopkins APL Technical Digest 19(4):482-492. Innes, M. (2018) unroll adjoint: differentiating SSA-form programs. arXiv:1810.07951. (Zygote.jl тАУ Phase 4b deferred.)

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters тАФ search_gradient
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_gradient(   default_specs(),   params        = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective     = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps       = 20L,   epsilon       = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1 ) result$specs$grass_rate } # }

what: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
text: Finds diverse archive high-performing parameter combinations using MAP-Elites algorithm (Mouret & Clune 2015). Rather finding one optimal parameter set, MAP-Elites maintains grid niches behaviour space; cell holds best-performing specs behavioural region.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
code: search_map_elites(   specs_base,   archive_dims,   n_iterations = 1000L,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   mutation_params = NULL,   mutation_sd = 0.1,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE,   checkpoint_path = NULL,   checkpoint_every = 100L )

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). Used starting point mutations. archive_dims named list whose names column names get_run_data()$ticks whose values numeric vectors specifying bin breakpoints dimension. Example: list(genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, = 0.1), n_species = 1:10). n_iterations Integer. Number MAP-Elites iterations (default 1000L). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise (e.g. "genetic_diversity"). function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. mutation_params Character vector parameter names (default_specs()) mutate. Defaults numeric parameters. mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations log-transformed parameter values (default 0.1). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores batch evaluation (default 1L). verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE). checkpoint_path Optional file path. supplied, current archive + history + iteration index saved RDS file every checkpoint_every iterations (end). path passed subsequent call, search resumes saved iteration. Set NULL (default) disable checkpointing. Added 0.5.6. checkpoint_every Integer. often write checkpoint, iterations (default 100L). Ignored checkpoint_path NULL. Added 0.5.6.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
text: list components: $archive list lists, one per archive cell, containing: $specs, $score, $behavioural_descriptor (named numeric vector). $map ggplot2 heatmap archive scores (2D archive dims ). NULL higher-dimensional archives. $history Data frame one row per iteration: iteration, score, filled_cells.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
text: Initialise: fill archive cell sampling random specs near specs_base. n_iterations iterations: . Select random filled cell archive. b. Mutate specs (Gaussian perturbation numeric parameters). c. Run run_alife(new_specs). d. Compute behavioural descriptors get_run_data(env). e. new specs score better current occupant corresponding archive cell, replace . Return full archive.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
text: Mouret, J.-B. & Clune, J. (2015) Illuminating search spaces mapping elites. arXiv:1504.04909. Chatzilygeroudis, K., Cully, ., Vassiliades, V. & Mouret, J.-B. (2021) Quality-Diversity Optimization: novel branch stochastic optimization. arXiv:2012.04322.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters тАФ search_map_elites
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_map_elites(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   archive_dims = list(     genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),     n_species         = 1:10   ),   n_iterations = 500L,   objective    = "genetic_diversity" ) result$map   # ggplot2 heatmap } # }

what: Stochastic parameter sweep for evolutionary outcome discovery тАФ search_random

title: Stochastic parameter sweep for evolutionary outcome discovery тАФ search_random

Example:   n_samples Integer. Number random configurations evaluate (default 50L). objective Character function. Column get_run_data()$ticks maximise (default "genetic_diversity"), function f(env) returning numeric scalar. verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE).
code: list(   mutation_sd   = c(0.01, 0.5),   grass_rate    = c(0.05, 0.8),   n_agents_init = c(10L, 200L) )

title: Stochastic parameter sweep for evolutionary outcome discovery тАФ search_random
text: data frame one row per sample, columns: rank Rank descending objective score (1 = best). score Objective value sample. ... One column per element search_params showing sampled value used. Attribute "specs_list" list full specs sample row, accessible via attr(result, "specs_list").

title: Stochastic parameter sweep for evolutionary outcome discovery тАФ search_random
text: Use search_random() : Screen parameters strongly influence genetic diversity. Identify high-diversity corners parameter space running expensive search_map_elites() search_cmaes(). Compare stochastic results MAP-Elites elites spot archive gaps.

title: Stochastic parameter sweep for evolutionary outcome discovery тАФ search_random
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Screen mutation_sd and grass_rate for highest genetic diversity result <- search_random(   specs_base    = default_specs(),   search_params = list(     mutation_sd   = c(0.01, 0.5),     grass_rate    = c(0.05, 0.8),     n_agents_init = c(10L, 150L)   ),   n_samples  = 30L,   objective  = "genetic_diversity" ) head(result)                          # top configurations plot(result$grass_rate, result$score) # partial dependence } # }

title: Grid-search parameter viability: which combinations allow population survival? тАФ search_viability

what: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
text: counterpart batch_alife() + summarize_batch() long sweeps: runs spec specs_list, extracts scalar summary per run via summary_fn, appends one CSV row out_path run completes. Two advantages batching:

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
code: stream_specs_to_csv(   specs_list,   out_path,   summary_fn = NULL,   n_cores = 1L,   resume = TRUE,   flush_every = 1L )

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
text: specs_list List specs. out_path Path CSV. Created absent; appended present. First row always header. summary_fn Function (env, specs) -> named list one-row data.frame. return summary stats want save per run. Default: viability verdict + n_final + mean_energy_final genetic_diversity_final + run's random_seed params differ default_specs(). n_cores Integer. Parallel workers, batch_alife(). resume Logical. TRUE (default) out_path already exists, skip runs whose run_id existing CSV. Set FALSE overwrite. flush_every Integer. often flush CSV disk (rows). Default 1 (flush every row тАФ safest; small /O cost).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
text: path CSV (invisibly). Read back read.csv(out_path).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv
text: Memory: full env object run discarded summary row written, 1 M-run sweep accumulate 1 M copies progress / deaths RAM. Resumability: job dies run 800 000 1 000 000, re-running out_path picks last written row (matched run_id column).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run тАФ stream_specs_to_csv

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep тАФ submit_sweep_slurm
code: submit_sweep_slurm(   specs_list,   out_path,   script_path,   rds_path,   n_array_tasks = min(100L, length(specs_list)),   n_cores_per_task = 4L,   time = "06:00:00",   mem = "8G",   summary_fn = NULL,   R_library_path = NULL,   extra_sbatch_lines = character(0) )

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep тАФ submit_sweep_slurm
text: specs_list List specs (e.g. sample_specs()). out_path Character. Path cluster filesystem CSV appended . Must reachable every node. script_path Character. Local path .sh file written. rds_path Character. Local path .rds specs_list written. Cluster nodes need able read path; shared filesystem just put somewhere cluster can see. n_array_tasks Integer. Number SLURM array tasks. gets ceiling(length(specs_list) / n_array_tasks) specs. Default: min(100, length(specs_list)). n_cores_per_task Integer. n_cores passed stream_specs_to_csv() within array task. Default 4L. time Character. SLURM --time value (e.g. "06:00:00"). mem Character. SLURM --mem value (e.g. "8G"). summary_fn Optional summary function, stream_specs_to_csv(). NULL (default), default summary used cluster side. R_library_path Optional character. Added .libPaths() cluster node via .libPaths(c("<path>", .libPaths())) library(clade). Useful clade installed non-standard location cluster. extra_sbatch_lines Character vector extra #SBATCH directives (without leading #SBATCH) include script preamble.

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep тАФ submit_sweep_slurm

what: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
text: Checks whether run_alife() result viable enough support claims evolved trait values. Population crashes (agents dying faster reproduce) silently corrupt trait-mean audits -weighting lucky survivors. function quantifies crash risk via three metrics returns tidy report together verdict {"viable", "weak", "crashed"}.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
code: viability_report(   run_data,   n_agents_init = NULL,   crashed_frac = 0.2,   weak_frac = 0.5,   min_n = 20L )

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
text: run_data list get_run_data() тАФ either single $ticks data frame full get_run_data() output. n_agents_init Integer. initial agent count used seed run. Required run_data carry spec. Pass NULL (default) use first-tick n_agents (approximates init-mean first wave births, usually close enough). crashed_frac Numeric (0, 1). run declared "crashed" n_final < crashed_frac * n_agents_init. Default 0.2 (final population less 20% init). weak_frac Numeric (0, 1). run declared "weak" (viable low confidence) n_final < weak_frac * n_agents_init. Default 0.5. min_n Integer. Absolute minimum n_final run "crashed" regardless crashed_frac. Default 20 (fewer 20 agents, trait mean dominated handful individuals). Set 0 disable floor.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
text: list : verdict One "viable", "weak", "crashed". n_init First-tick n_agents used reference. n_final Last-tick n_agents. n_min Minimum n_agents across whole run. frac_final n_final / n_init. frac_min n_min / n_init. tick_of_min First tick n_min reached. message one-line diagnostic suitable logging.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
text: Motivation: 2026-04-17 fast_specs re-audit s-plasticity s-dispersal-ifd, direction flips 5-seed multi-seed results traced seasonal runs n_final < 20 stable runs maintained healthy populations. trait-mean average 0-5 surviving agents dominated specific crash trajectory rather evolutionary signal. utility codifies "check n_final trusting trait-mean effects" rule reusable check.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run тАФ viability_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ env  <- run_alife(fast_specs()) vr   <- viability_report(get_run_data(env)) print(vr) # Guard audit claims on viability: if (vr$verdict == "crashed") {   warning("crash-driven result; trait means are unreliable") } } # }

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: Substantial multi-PR day covering timescale presets, ЁЯЯа-sort work, new audit-methodology utility, working parallel scenario-search toolkit, three kernel bug fixes, head--head brain-type benchmark. 22+ PRs merged main.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: story: existing batch_alife(n_cores = N) path silently deadlocked (forked R workers blocked one Julia socket тАФ fork-unsafe RPC). Fix: swap parallel::mclapply parallel::makeCluster("PSOCK"), worker separate R process Julia session. fix applied search_cmaes. n_cores > 1 now actually parallelises. New exports built fixed parallel path: grid_specs(base, ...) тАФ factorial expansion base spec. sample_specs(base, n, ...) тАФ random draws three distribution forms (vector тЖТ sample replacement; list 2 тЖТ uniform; function тЖТ user-supplied). summarize_batch(results, specs_list) тАФ one-row-per-run tidy data frame parameters, default metrics, viability verdict. stream_specs_to_csv(specs_list, out_path) тАФ memory-efficient streaming writer 10kтАУ1M-scenario sweeps. resume = TRUE (default) skips run_ids already output CSV, killed overnight jobs restart cleanly. submit_sweep_slurm() тАФ writes SLURM array-job template dispatches chunks specs_list across cluster nodes, task calling stream_specs_to_csv() shared CSV. search_map_elites(checkpoint_path, checkpoint_every) тАФ checkpoint/resume long MAP-Elites runs. New dedicated vignette: parameter-space-search.Rmd (placed parameter-reference sidebar) walks full workflow small grids million-scenario cluster sweeps. Full deadlock post-mortem: dev/docs/parallelism-audit.md.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: body_size foraging death spiral: apply_body_size! charged small agents (bs < 1) foraging correction bounded 40% current energy тАФ low-grass cells correction exceeded eat_gain, making eating net-negative. Small agents drifted bs тЙИ 0.5 starved. Fix: scale eat_gain * bite * body_size source tick.jl; drop post-hoc correction. realistic mutation_sd (тЙд 0.02) scenario now viable fast_specs. RNG-order contamination BNN sampler: randn(length(mu)) Thompson sampling used JuliaтАЩs global RNG. Consecutive run_alife() calls one Julia session shared state figures depended call order even random_seed set. Fix: per-run RNG cache populated env.rng top tick_agents!. Verified deterministic across call order. search_cmaes parallel path: PSOCK fix batch_alife. Single cluster reused across generations amortise Julia compile cost.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: bnn_action_noise_scale (0.5.5, now documented): decouples BNN sigma action sampling. scale = 0, actions deterministic mu; sigma affects learning/cost. action_exploration_epsilon (0.5.6): epsilon-greedy exploration orthogonal BNN sigma. epsilon > 0, probability tick picks uniformly random action. Intended exploration channel sigma fully decoupled. bnn_sigma_lr_scale, bnn_sigma_lr_ref (0.5.6): within-life effective learning rate scales mean(sigma)/sigma_ref. Canalised agents learn slowly; plastic agents learn fast. Puts cost canalisation learning speed, noise. Combined, three levers give 3-axis decoupling sigma channel. None individually promoted Baldwin тЬЕ тАФ baseline sigma dynamics clade donтАЩt canalise fast_specs settings, levers never engage. Filed clearest next step dev/audit/fidelity/ORANGE_OVERVIEW.md.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: viability_report() тАФ new exported function flags runs n_final < 20 n_final < 0.2 ├Ч n_init. run_alife() now attaches env$viability warns "crashed" verdicts. Codifies тАЬalways check viability interpreting trait meansтАЭ rule audit round kept relearning. Cross-scenario crash audit (crash_audit.R): 17 scenarios ├Ч 5 seeds fast_specs revealed 4 тЬЕ scenarios crash fast_specs (body_size, signals, parental_care, stress_hypermutation). morningтАЩs demo-chunk updates silently reverted four keep default_specs. Evidence-strength review (EVIDENCE_REVIEW.md): tiers 30 auditable scenarios Strong / Moderate / Weak-тЬЕ / Honest ЁЯЯа. Revealed 14 26 тЬЕ sit Tier C (audited pre-8-seed discipline). Tier C re-audit (batches 1 + 2): ran 12 14 Tier C scenarios ├Ч 8 seeds ├Ч 2 conditions. Six pass module-firing-correctness checks (cooperation, speciation, niche, parental_care, complex_landscape, seasonal). Five demoted: group_defense, social_learning, scavenging, brain_size, rl тАФ canonical theoretical claim doesnтАЩt hold 8 seeds. One marginal (parental_investment). Inventory next steps 9 ЁЯЯа dev/audit/fidelity/ORANGE_OVERVIEW.md.

title: clade 0.5.4 (2026-04-16)
text: Applied 0.5.3 methodology (adequate seeds + 2├ЧSE testing start) 8-cell parameter search regime produces statistically clean upward toxicity evolution full 0.4.4 vector-signal machinery. Result: every tested regime shows ╬Фtoxicity < 0 (range тИТ0.002 тИТ0.009) across 40 total runs. selection arithmetic: Toxicity cost: 0.03тАУ0.06 energy/tick (cost ├Ч mean toxicity) Aposematic protection: 9тАУ23 avoidance events per 1000 ticks across ~2000 attacks = ~1% predator attacks toxic prey blocked. Net effect: cost dominates benefit ~10├Ч default population scales (n=100, 12тАУ20 predators). Zahavi (1975) handicap-honesty challenge ABM scale тАФ mirrors 0.5.3 Red Queen finding (HamiltonтАЩs two-fold cost sex) real simulation-ecology limitation canonical mechanism correct cost--benefit ratio default clade kernel doesnтАЩt support evolution theory predicts. s-mimicry stays ЁЯЯа kernel machinery confirmed correct ecology-limitation documented. Deferred 0.6+ scenario design question (smaller populations, intense predation, bootstrapping correlated signal + toxicity). kernel changes.

what: Audit: s-mating-systems 0.5.1 тАЬfirst sex > asexтАЭ claim retracted
title: clade 0.5.3 (2026-04-16)
text: Applied 0.5.2 body-size precedent (16-seed sweep 2├ЧSE hypothesis test) Red Queen scenario. Across 448 audit runs covering 19 distinct parameter regimes: 16-seed replication 0.5.1 default parasite_discrete parameters: ╬Фn (sex тИТ asex) = тИТ0.49 ┬▒ 1.54 тЖТ flat within 2├ЧSE. 0.5.1 тАЬ+1.1 first sex winsтАЭ finding 3-seed noise. Regime search n_loci ├Ч pressure ├Ч exponent ├Ч mutation (16 cells, 8 seeds ): 16 regimes show direction favour sex average; NONE crosses 2├ЧSE. Top-3 verification 16 seeds: 8-seed apparent signals (+2.3 +2.8) collapse flat ╬Фn тИИ {тИТ1.07, +0.42, тИТ0.45}. canonical discrete-allele Red Queen mechanism implemented correctly (direction consistently non-negative average), cladeтАЩs baseline cost sex higher parasite selection pressure can offset every tested regime. Hamilton (1980) noted sexтАЩs two-fold cost tall order parasites; finding consistent caveat. Verdict: s-mating-systems stays ЁЯЯа direction correct тАЬfirst sex winsтАЭ claim retracted. Pushing тЬЕ deferred scenario-design question (minimise cladeтАЩs baseline cost sex via population size, mate-finding dynamics, longer runs). kernel changes.

title: clade 0.5.2 (2026-04-16)
text: 16-seed ├Ч 2-sensing-mode ├Ч 2-predator-level factorial replaces earlier 5-seed audits. P1 (Cope direction) robust +9тАУ11% upward drift (SE тЙд 0.7% effect). P2 (predation direction) NULL sensing modes тАФ neither Shine-acceleration (0.4.3 framing) Brooks-Dodson-detectability (0.4.1 framing) statistically supported default parameters. direction claims retracted. Side finding: 0.4.2 graded predator sensing produces larger Cope drift (+0.107) legacy binary sensing (+0.087), SE-bounded real effect finer threat information improving foraging efficiency. kernel changes.

title: clade 0.5.1 (2026-04-16)
text: 0.5.0тАЩs continuous-trait parasite module produce HamiltonтАЩs canonical Red Queen тАФ sex offspring midpoints parents cluster near parasite optimum, sex exposed. 0.5.1 adds discrete-allele variant reproduces textbook effect. New Agent field parasite_haplotype::Vector{Int32} (heritable binary haplotype length n_parasite_loci). Mendelian inheritance free recombination: diploid offspring inherit locus independently either parent, producing genuinely novel haplotype combinations. Haploid clone + per-locus mutation. Hamming-distance matching parasite module: per-host penalty = pressure ├Ч ((n_loci тИТ hamming) / n_loci)^exponent. Hosts exactly matching parasite haplotype pay full pressure; mismatched hosts escape cleanly. New specs: n_parasite_loci (default 0L), parasite_match_mode ("auto" / "continuous" / "discrete"), parasite_mutation_rate (0.01), parasite_discrete_exponent (4.0).

title: clade 0.3.0
text: Batesian mimicry (specs$batesian_mimicry = TRUE). palatable mimic (toxicity = 0) whose signal matches toxic model species now benefits predatorтАЩs learned aversion тАФ predator avoids mimic alongside true toxic prey. Repeated attacks palatable mimics decay predatorтАЩs aversion memory (Rescorla-Wagner toward 0), reproducing BatesтАУWallace predator discrimination-learning cycle. Default FALSE тАФ existing runs byte-identical. Reference: Bates (1862) Trans. Linn. Soc. 23:495тАУ566; Ruxton, Sherratt & Speed (2004) Avoiding Attack. Heritable niche-construction benefit (specs$shelter_occupancy_bonus > 0). Agents occupying sheltered cell receive bonus ├Ч depth energy per tick, Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) heritable-environment effect. Existing niche_construction semantics (grass suppression + persistence + decay) unchanged; new parameter additive defaults 0 (behavior change).

Predator dedicated sensory architecture. seed_predators! now builds predator-specific brain architecture [15, hidden..., 5] brainтАЩs n_inputs matches 15-element predator sense vector. Previously predators used preyтАЩs architecture (dynamic size depending input_radius active sensory modules), producing silent dimension mismatch whenever defaults changed. Mutation-rate evolution uses per-agent trait. mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE, reproduce.jl now reads parent.mutation_sd base mutation rate meiosis. Without drift, signals remained exactly 0 forever, making mate-choice experiments uninformative.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: Lamarckian evolution (lamarckian = TRUE): rl_mode "none", within-lifetime RL-updated brain weights written back parentтАЩs genome meiosis offspring inherit learned solution directly. Implemented inst/julia/src/modules/lamarckian.jl. Distinct epigenetics module (inherits methylation marks, weight values) Baldwin Effect (leaves genome unchanged). References: Baldwin (1896); Weismann (1892); Jablonka & Lamb (2005). Discrete / quantized ANN weights (ann_weight_values): set numeric vector (e.g.┬аc(-1, 0, 1) ternary weights), every synaptic weight bias snapped nearest allowed value genome expression. Applies "ann" "bnn" brain types. Biologically motivated evidence biological synapses operate discrete strength states (Bhumbra & Bhatt 2020). Enables symbolic formula distillation evolved ANNs (original MATLAB alife2025usra codebase). ANN weight regularisation (ann_regularization): per-tick energy penalty brain weight complexity. Two modes: "weight_magnitude" (L1 penalty, drives weights toward zero) "weight_count" (L0-like penalty, fixed cost per active synapse). Scaled ann_regularization_lambda (default 0.001). mean weight magnitude now logged every run mean_ann_weight_magnitude. References: Laughlin et al.┬а(1998); Attwell & Laughlin (2001). Native Julia test directory (inst/julia/test/): unit tests quantization, regularisation, Lamarckian logic can run directly Julia without R side. Run julia --project=inst/julia inst/julia/test/runtests.jl. Module triage script (inst/scripts/triage_modules.R): runs module isolation 300 ticks reports whether key biological signal detected ([OK]), absent ([FLAT]), crashing ([ERR]). Run Rscript inst/scripts/triage_modules.R. expect_evolution() test helper (tests/testthat/helper.R): asserts logged trait moves directionally simulation run. Also consolidates duplicated skip_no_julia() definition helper.R individual test files longer need re-define . Non-toroidal grid (toroidal = FALSE): Julia modules now use wrap_or_clamp() helper either wraps (toroidal) clamps grid boundary (linear). Required spatial sorting invasion-front experiments defined front. Carrion pathogen reservoir (carrion_transmission_prob): scavenging = TRUE disease = TRUE, agents die infected deposit flagged carcass; agent scavenges becomes infected probability carrion_transmission_prob (default 0.0 тАФ backwards compatibility). batch_seeds(): convenience wrapper around batch_alife() takes single specs object vector seeds returns named list results. quick_specs() / full_specs(): preset specs fast exploratory runs (50 agents, 200 ticks, 20├Ч20 grid) publication-quality runs (200 agents, 1000 ticks, 30├Ч30 grid).

title: clade 0.1.0
text: First public release.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: Agent-based evolutionary simulation running entirely Julia via JuliaConnectoR, eliminating per-tick R/Julia boundary overhead. Toroidal grid logistic grass regrowth seasonal amplitude modulation. Diploid haploid genome meiosis, Mendelian dominance models, configurable crossover mutation rates. Six brain types: Bayesian neural network (BNN, default), multilayer perceptron (ANN), continuous-time RNN (CTRNN), gene regulatory network (GRN), random null model, stubs transformer synthesis brains. run_alife() тАФ single simulation run; returns named list compatible get_run_data() plot_run(). batch_alife() тАФ run multiple replicates sequence. default_specs() тАФ fully documented parameter list sensible defaults.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: module -op flag FALSE; overhead zero. Disease / SIR (disease = TRUE): stochastic transmission, energy costs, recovery, immunity. Kin selection (kin_selection = TRUE): pedigree-based relatedness; donors transfer energy -related Moore-neighbourhood agent relatedness threshold. Cooperation (cooperation = TRUE): reciprocal altruism mediated heritable cooperation-level trait. Scavenging (scavenging = TRUE): carrion deposited agent death; scavengers gain energy decaying carcasses. Niche construction (niche_construction = TRUE): agents build shelters slow grass regrowth reduce predator damage; shelters decay stochastically. Epigenetics (epigenetics = TRUE): heritable methylation marks canalize BNN weight uncertainty (sigma); transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) transmits marks offspring configurable probability. Within-lifetime RL (rl_mode = "actor_critic"): REINFORCE baseline updates output layer agentтАЩs neural network tick, driven energy-gain reward. Social learning (social_learning = TRUE): prestige-biased copying тАФ agents blend fraction highest-energy neighbourтАЩs output-layer weights policy. Brain size evolution (brain_size_evolution = TRUE): heritable brain_size trait (Float32, reference 1.0) modelling parental provisioning hypothesis (van Schaik et al.┬а2023; Griesser et al.┬а2023; Song et al.┬а2025). Larger brains incur per-tick idle-cost surcharge (expensive brain hypothesis) proportional cognitive foraging bonus. bootstrapping problem тАФ large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth foraging advantage emerges тАФ means brain size evolves parental_care = TRUE buffers infancy energy deficit. third effect тАФ sensing quality тАФ scales grass perception inputs brain_size ^ brain_size_sensing_exponent, giving larger-brained agents directional navigation advantage. Logged mean_brain_size env$progress. New parameters: brain_size_evolution, brain_size_init_mean, brain_size_mutation_sd, brain_size_min, brain_size_max, brain_size_cost_scale, brain_size_sensing_exponent.
